question: What was Samson’s major achievement as Israel’s leader?
solution: Samson was a judge in Israel whose primary mission was to deliver the Israelites from the oppression of the Philistines. His strength was a divine endowment for this specific purpose (Judges 13-16).

options.b: He captured the city of Jericho
options.c: He saved the Israelites from the Amorites
options.d: He secured permanent independence for Israel

question: “........ I am the Lord, and there is no other, besides me there is no God....” God’s statement above through Isaiah depicts
solution: The statement 'I am the Lord, and there is no other' (Isaiah 45:5) emphasizes God's unique divine nature and absolute monotheism, declaring that He alone is God.

options.a: assurance to Isaiah of His existence

question: “Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much evil he has done to thy saints at Jerusalem...” Ananias’ response above to God’s request for him to meet Saul shows that he
solution: Ananias voiced concerns about Saul because of his reputation for persecuting 'saints at Jerusalem'. This reflects his fear and initial reluctance based on Saul's notorious past (Acts 9:13).

options.a: felt that Saul should not be forgiven
options.b: felt that Saul should be punished
options.c: was not interested in meeting Saul

question: God’s constant love for unfaithful Israel is demonstrated in the book of
solution: The book of Hosea uses the prophet's marriage to an unfaithful wife (Gomer) as a powerful metaphor for God's constant and redeeming love for an unfaithful Israel.

question: One of the following is not a quality of good discipleship_________
solution: A quality described as 'exuberance' (excessive energy/excitement) is not explicitly listed as a biblical requirement for discipleship, whereas self-denial and commitment are core tenets (Matthew 16:24).

question: Peter described Christians as a chosen race of God  because they are to
solution: In 1 Peter 2:9, Christians are called a 'chosen race' and a 'royal priesthood' specifically so that they may 'declare the wonderful deeds' of Him who called them out of darkness.

options.d: displace the Jews in God’s rating

question: The descent of the Holy Spirit on the day of  Pentecost was prophesied by
solution: The prophet Joel (Joel 2:28-29) famously prophesied that God would 'pour out my Spirit on all flesh,' a prophecy fulfilled on the day of Pentecost (Acts 2).

question: Which one of the following is the man associated with prayers?
solution: Elijah is prominently associated with powerful and effective prayer, notably for the rain to stop and then start again, and for fire on Mount Carmel (James 5:17-18).

question: Who was the king who reintroduced the passover feast in
solution: King Josiah is celebrated for his religious reforms, which included the reintroduction of the Passover feast after discovering the Book of the Law (2 Kings 23).

question: Who among the following kings was raised by God against the Israelites for going after the Canaanite gods?
solution: As a punishment for Israel's idolatry, God raised up Jabin, king of Canaan, to oppress them for twenty years before Deborah and Barak delivered them (Judges 4).

question: Who is reputed for pulling down the altar of Baal at the risk of annoying his father?
solution: Gideon, also known as Jerubbaal, is famous for destroying his father's altar of Baal and the Asherah pole beside it, signaling his commitment to Yahweh (Judges 6).

question: St. Paul showed commitment as a means upon which forgiveness can effectively thrive by_______
solution: In his epistle to Philemon, Paul demonstrated deep commitment by offering to personally pay any debts owed by Onesimus, thus facilitating true reconciliation (Philemon 1:18-19).

options.a: Appealing to Philemon to forgive Onesimus and accept him back as brother
options.b: Asking that Philemon to Charge to his account whatever Onesimus owed
options.d: Instructing Philemon to write off all the debts of Onesimus, if any compassionately

question: According to Jesus, a good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep but a hireling
solution: Jesus distinguishes the 'Good Shepherd' from a 'hireling' by noting that the hireling 'abandons the sheep and runs away' when he sees a wolf coming, because he doesn't own them (John 10:12).

options.a: sells them off for personal profit
options.b: abandons the sheep in the face of danger
options.c: protects some of the sheep leaving others to danger
options.d: asks for better pay before doing his work.

question: According to Peter, doing the right thing is a way of expressing
solution: In the teachings attributed to Peter (and James), doing the right thing and living righteously are acts that express one's deeper 'faith in Christ' and gratitude for salvation.

question: Paul was stoned and dragged out of the city by
solution: During Paul's first missionary journey, Jews from Antioch and Iconium came to Lystra, won over the crowds, and stoned Paul, dragging him out of the city (Acts 14:19).

options.b: enemies of the healed lame man.

question: Prophet Hosea named his second child Not Pitied because God would no more pity the
solution: Hosea 1:6 states that God commanded the prophet to name his second child 'Lo-ruhamah' (Not Pitied) because He would no longer have pity on the house of Israel.

question: Obadiah, Ahabs servant, made history for himself by
solution: Obadiah, the steward for King Ahab, was a devout believer who hid 100 prophets of Yahweh in two caves and fed them, protecting them from Jezebel's persecution (1 Kings 18:4).

options.c: hiding and feeding some of the prophets in a cave.
options.d: inciting the prophets to rebel against king Ahab.

question: “But I say to you, do not resist one who is evil.” This was part of Jesus’ teaching on
solution: Jesus taught 'do not resist one who is evil' as part of his Sermon on the Mount (Matthew 5:38-39), specifically addressing the old law of 'an eye for an eye' (retaliation).

question: According to Corinthians, variety of gifts are inspired by
solution: 1 Corinthians 12 explains that while there are various spiritual gifts (wisdom, healing, prophecy), they are all activated and inspired by 'the same Spirit' for the common good.

question: ‘Truly, I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise.....’ Jesus made the statement above to one of the 		criminals because he (Luke 23:42-43)
solution: On the cross, one of the criminals rebuked the other and acknowledged Jesus' innocence and lordship. His 'repentant' heart led Jesus to promise him paradise (Luke 23:42-43).

question: Who was the King of Judah who witnessed the second deportation?
solution: After the death of Solomon, Rehoboam was king. Following the division of the kingdom, King Zedekiah was the king of Judah who witnessed the final destruction of Jerusalem and the second major deportation (2 Kings 25).

question: The treasures which Jesus wants us to lay in heaven include
solution: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus teaches that true treasures are not material wealth like 'gold and silver' but 'works of righteousness' and godly character that endure eternally (Matthew 6:19-21).

question: "For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice..." this statement relates to the_______
solution: Hosea 6:6 ('I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice') reflects God's response to Israel's superficial and inconsistent show of love, emphasizing that inner faithfulness matters more than external rituals.

options.a: response of God's love to the children of Israel
options.b: response of Israel to the genuineness of God's love
options.c: message of God's love to man
options.d: message of Hosea on faithfulness and social progress

question: Peter’s general outlook towards Gentile converts was significantly changed by the conversion of
solution: The conversion of Cornelius, a Roman centurion (Acts 10), was a watershed moment for Peter. God used a vision and this specific event to teach Peter that Gentiles were also welcome in the kingdom.

question: The boldness of Peter and John before the Sanhedrin was attributed to
solution: The Sanhedrin in Acts 4 was astonished by the boldness of Peter and John, who were uneducated men. They recognized that this courage and power came from 'their having been with Jesus'.

question: The relationship between God and Israel was described by Hosea as that of a
solution: Hosea 2:2-5 uses a faithless wife (Gomer) as a metaphor for Israel's unfaithfulness to God, who is represented as a loving and forgiving husband (the bond of the covenant).

options.a: faithless wife and a loving husband
options.b: a loving wife and a faithless husband
options.c: a penitent wife and aggressive husband
options.d: a faithless husband and a faithless and nonchalant wife.

question: God had pity on the people of Nineveh because
solution: The book of Jonah records that God had mercy on Nineveh specifically because the people turned from their evil ways and repented after hearing Jonah's message (Jonah 3:10).

options.b: the king punished all the violent people in the land
options.c: they turned from their evil way.
options.d: they made sacrifices of sin offering to God.

question: How did the Ninevites react to Jonah’s message?
solution: When Jonah finally preached in Nineveh, the people, from the greatest to the least, believed God's word and 'repented of their sins', leading to God's mercy (Jonah 3).

options.b: They believed it for a while
options.d: They ordered him out of their city

question: Pilate referred Jesus to Herod for trial because
solution: In Luke 23, Pilate sent Jesus to Herod Antipas for trial because Jesus was a Galilean and thus 'lived in the jurisdiction of Herod', who was in Jerusalem for the Passover.

options.a: Pilate and Herod were good friends
options.b: Jesus did not cooperate with Pilate.
options.c: Jesus lived in the jurisdiction of Herod.

question: One of the following is not among the reasons why Jesus taught in Parables
solution: Jesus taught in parables to fulfill prophecy, common wisdom, and to make the unknown known. However, He also stated in Mark 4:11-12 that for those on the 'outside', it was to keep them from fully seeing and understanding—making B ('to create understanding') the incorrect choice in this context.

options.a: to fulfill the prophecy in Psalm 78:2
options.c: to make people learn the unknown through the known
options.d: to show the omniscient power of Jesus

question: What effect did the decision of the Council of Jerusalem have on the early Church?
solution: The Council of Jerusalem (Acts 15) was pivotal because it officially decided that 'Gentiles were free to be members of the Church' without having to strictly adhere to all Jewish laws like circumcision.

options.b: the Church experienced freedom from persecution
options.c: The Jews became leaders of the community.
options.d: Gentiles were free to be members of the Church

question: In Paul’s epistle to the Thessalonians, any Christian living in idleness should be
solution: In 2 Thessalonians 3:6, Paul instructs Christians to 'keep away from every brother who is idle and does not live according to the teaching' passed on to them.

question: In Corinthians, Paul cited all the following resurrection appearances of Jesus except
solution: In 1 Corinthians 15, Paul lists several of Jesus' post-resurrection appearances (Cephas, the Twelve, 500 brothers, James). Mary Magdalene is notably 'excepted' from his specific list of formal witnesses in this letter.

question: The message in the parable of the weeds is to
solution: The Parable of the Weeds (Matthew 13) warns Christians to 'be aware of false teaching' and the presence of the 'enemy' (Satan) who plants counterfeits among the true followers of Christ.

options.a: make Christians to be aware of the evil one
options.c: teach Christians to be aware of false teaching
options.d: warn Christians on what will happen at the close of age

question: By introducing Jesus as the Lamb, John was pointing to
solution: John the Baptist's introduction of Jesus as 'the Lamb of God' (John 1:29) points directly to Jesus' future 'sacrificial death' which would take away the sin of the world.

question: Paul in Thessalonians declared that at the second coming of the Lord, those who are alive will not precede those that are
solution: In 1 Thessalonians 4:15, Paul consoles believers by stating that at the Lord's coming, those who are alive will certainly not precede those who have 'fallen asleep' (died) in Christ.

question: The miracles of Jesus recorded in John’s gospel are called
solution: In John's Gospel, miracles are consistently referred to as 'signs' (Semeion) because they point beyond the physical act to the deeper identity and glory of Jesus as the Son of God.

question: The Hebrew midwives who obeyed God rather than Pharaoh were blessed with
solution: In Exodus 1:17-21, the Hebrew midwives (Shiphrah and Puah) feared God and disobeyed Pharaoh's order to kill male babies. As a result, God was kind to them and 'gave them families of their own'.

question: The concept of  forgiveness does not include
solution: The biblical concept of forgiveness is deeply rooted in 'reconciliation', 'restoration', and 'peace' with others. It is a spiritual and relational act, not one associated with 'material wealth'.

question: During the Ammonite war Uriah disobeyed king David by refusing to go to his house to sleep because
solution: During the Ammonite war, Uriah the Hittite refused to go to his home to sleep with his wife because the Ark and the army were in tents. He felt it would be 'unpatriotic to break his military vow' of solidarity (2 Samuel 11).

options.a: it was wrong to go home drunk.
options.b: his wife would regard him as a cowar
options.c: it would be unpatroitic to break military vow.
options.d: he suspected that the king had lost interest in the battle

question: Before his ascension, Jesus commissioned his disciples to go to all nations and
solution: Before His ascension, Jesus gave the 'Great Commission' to His disciples (Matthew 28:19-20), commanding them to go to all nations and 'make disciples' by baptizing and teaching them.

question: “... you brood of vipers!  who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?” The “wrath to come” here refers to the
solution: The 'wrath to come' mentioned by John the Baptist (Matthew 3:7) refers to the 'judgement day' when all humanity will answer for their deeds and repentance will be required.

question: Which of the following parables alludes to the existence of a mixture of good and bad people in the church? The
solution: The Parable of the 'Wheat and Tares' (Matthew 13) describes the co-existence of 'children of the kingdom' and 'children of the evil one' in the world (and church) until the final harvest.

question: The son of Solomon who made unwise decisions was ….
solution: Rehoboam, the son of Solomon, is renowned for receiving advice from both elders and his younger peers. He chose the latter's 'unwise decisions', leading to the division of Israel (1 Kings 12).

question: Cleopas' heart burnt within him on the road to Emmaus when Jesus ________
solution: On the road to Emmaus (Luke 24), the disciples' hearts 'burnt within them' as the unrecognized Jesus 'explained the scriptures' to them, revealing how the Messiah had to suffer.

question: “It is necessary to circumcise them, and to charge them to keep the law of Moses.” In response to the Pharisees’ statement above, Peter rebuked them, by saying that God has
solution: During the Council of Jerusalem, Peter argued that God had 'cleansed the hearts of the Gentiles by faith' (Acts 15:9), showing that physical circumcision was no longer the mark of God's people.

options.a: made circumcision for only those in leadership
options.b: chosen the Israelites among other people
options.c: cleansed the Gentiles’ heart by faith
options.d: put the yoke upon the Gentiles’ necks.

question: Which two tribes in Israel led in the rebuilding of the temple after the exile?
solution: After the decree of Cyrus, the tribes of 'Judah and Benjamin' (Ezra 1:5) alongside the priests and Levites, led the return to Jerusalem to rebuild the house of the Lord.

question: When Saul and his troops came after David in the wilderness of Ziph, David and Abishai went to Saul’s camp to
solution: When Saul was pursuing David, David and Abishai bravely entered Saul's camp at night while they were 'asleep' (1 Samuel 26). David refused to slay Saul, showing respect for God's anointed.

question: The presentation of Myrrh to Jesus signifies Jesus’
solution: The wise men's gift of 'myrrh' (an embalming spice) was symbolic of Jesus' humanity and His future 'suffering and impending death' for the sins of the world (Matthew 2).

question: The initial evidence that the disciples had received the Holy Spirit was
solution: On the day of Pentecost (Acts 2), the initial and visible evidence of the Holy Spirit's arrival was the disciples 'speaking in other tongues' as the Spirit gave them utterance.

question: The lesson which Jesus teaches in the parable of the Good Samaritan is that your neighbor is
solution: In the Parable of the Good Samaritan (Luke 10:25-37), Jesus teaches that your neighbor is not just your kinsman, but 'anyone who needs your help', regardless of their background.

question: Paul told the believers in Corinth that no one can say Jesus is Lord except by
solution: In 1 Corinthians 12:3, Paul teaches that no one can sincerely declare 'Jesus is Lord' except by the power and revelation of 'the Holy Spirit' working in their heart.

question: Darius became convinced of God’s power through Daniel’s
solution: King Darius was moved by Daniel's 'faithfulness and steadfastness' (Daniel 6) in the face of the lions' den, leading him to proclaim the power of the living God.

options.c: fearlessness in the face of difficulties.

question: ‘... But let justice roll down like waters, and righteousness like an overflowing stream.’ Amos’ statement above suggests that the society was terribly
solution: The prophet Amos (Amos 5:24) cried out for justice and righteousness to roll down because he saw an 'oppressive' society where the rich exploited the poor and denied them justice.

question: ‘Death is swallowed up in victory, “oh death, where is your victory”? This statement is found in the book of
solution: The triumphant declaration 'Death is swallowed up in victory' is a central theme in Paul's teaching on the resurrection, found in the book of '1 Corinthians' (specifically 15:54).

question: According to Paul in Romans, the law and the prophets bore witness to the righteousness of God but man could not be justified until he
solution: In Romans 3, Paul explains that while the law points to sin, justification and righteousness only come through 'faith in Jesus' Christ to all who believe.

question: The ruler of the synagogue whose child Jesus raised to life was
solution: Jairus, a ruler of the synagogue, famously pleaded with Jesus to heal his daughter. Jesus subsequently 'raised her to life' (Mark 5, Luke 8) after performing other miracles along the way.

question: According to Joseph’s interpretation to Pharaoh’s dreams; the seven empty ears blighted by the east wind represented seven years of
solution: In the book of Genesis, Joseph interprets Pharaoh's dreams of seven lean cows and seven 'blighted ears' as representing seven years of severe 'famine' following seven years of plenty.

question: During the creative activity of God, He made the beasts
solution: Genesis 1:24-25 records that God created the beasts of the earth and cattle 'according to their kinds', and He saw that it was good during the week of creation.

options.d: to serve the needs of man

question: Jesus came to John at River Jordan to
solution: Jesus came to the River Jordan to 'receive baptism like others' (Matthew 3:13-17) not for the remission of sin, but to identify with humanity and 'fulfill all righteousness'.

question: Only ...stated that the transfiguration took place eight days after Peter's confession of Jesus as the son of God.
solution: In the Synoption Gospels, only Luke (9:28) states that the Transfiguration occurred 'about eight days' after Peter's confession, while Matthew and Mark mention six days.

question: Eli, the priest in Shiloh, was condemned because he
solution: Eli was condemned because he honored his sons more than God by failing to restrain them from their corrupt behavior and for not taking God’s warnings through Samuel seriously (1 Samuel 3:13).

options.a: allowed his children to deprive God of His share of sacrifice.
options.b: allowed the Ark of God to be taken to the battle field
options.c: did not take God’s message through Samuel seriously.
options.d: officiated at the temple without the assistance of the prophet.

question: According to Peter, Judgment of God must first begin in
solution: In 1 Peter 4:17, the apostle teaches that 'it is time for judgment to begin with the family of God,' emphasizing that believers are held to a high standard of accountability.

question: Joshua’s first victory was over the people of
solution: After crossing the Jordan, Joshua's first major military victory was the miraculous collapse of the walls of Jericho, which allowed Israel to capture the city (Joshua 6).

question: When the people of Israel received the minority report from the spies, they decided to
solution: Upon hearing the discouraging report from the majority of the spies, the Israelites panicked and expressed a desire to 'choose a leader and go back to Egypt' (Numbers 14).

options.a: kill Moses for taking them out of Egypt
options.c: choose Caleb as their new leader
options.d: stone the two spies to death.

question: How did God guide Eleazar in choosing a wife for lsaac?
solution: In Genesis 24, Abraham's servant (presumably Eleazar) was guided by God's angel to Rebekah after he prayed for a specific sign involving water for himself and his camels.

options.a: He caused Rebekah to give him free accommodation
options.b: He made Rebekah’s parents willing to release her
options.c: He caused His angel to go ahead of him
options.d: He revealed to Rebekah before Eleazar’s arrival

question: The First Martyr among the apostles was
solution: While Stephen was the first martyr of the Jesus movement (Acts 7), James (the brother of John) was the first of the twelve apostles to be martyred, executed by Herod Agrippa (Acts 12:2).

question: Jeremiah said the people of Judah were in a state of mourning because,
solution: Jeremiah frequently lamented that Judah's land was in mourning and their farms were destroyed because of a severe drought sent as a sign of judgment for their sins (Jeremiah 14).

options.d: there was drought in the land.

question: According to Paul in Philippians, humility means giving consideration to other people’s
solution: In Philippians 2:4, Paul defines humility and Christian love as 'not looking to your own interests but each of you to the interests of the others.'

question: Who was the prophet responsible for the re-building of the Temple during the exile?
solution: Ezra was the priest and scribe who returned from Babylon to lead the spiritual rebuilding of the Temple and the community through the teaching of the Law (Ezra 7).

question: Who was one of the Seven Deacons who had four daughters who were prophetesses?
solution: In Acts 21:8-9, Philip the evangelist (one of the original seven deacons) is mentioned as having 'four unmarried daughters who prophesied.'

question: In God’s covenant with Israel, the central theme was
solution: In the Mosaic covenant, the central and recurring theme is 'obedience' to God's commandments as the foundation for the relationship between Yahweh and Israel (Exodus 19-20).

question: A good example of parental responsibility was demonstrated by
solution: Asa is a classic biblical example of parental responsibility because he removed the pagan altars and taught his son Jehoshaphat to follow God (1 Kings 15). Eli, by contrast, is a 'failure' for not restraining his wicked sons.

question: Hosanna means
solution: The Hebrew word 'Hosanna' (Hoshana) used during Jesus' triumphal entry literally means 'Save us, we pray' or 'Save now' (Psalm 118:25).

question: John the Baptist describes Jesus to his two disciples as the
solution: In John 1:29 and 1:36, John the Baptist looks at Jesus and declares, 'Behold, the Lamb of God!' pointing to His role as the ultimate sacrifice for sin.

question: James teaches that the man who shall be blessed is he who
solution: In James 1:25, the apostle teaches that the person who will be blessed is the one who is 'not a forgetful hearer but a doer who acts.'

options.d: teaches the word of God well.

question: What was Herod’s reaction when he heard the news about Jesus’ birth?
solution: Matthew 2:3 records that when King Herod heard about the birth of the 'King of the Jews' from the Magi, 'he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him' due to his fear of a rival.

question: Paul believes that spiritual gifts are of great advantage to the Church because they
solution: Paul teaches in 1 Corinthians 12-14 that spiritual gifts are given for the 'common good' and are intended to 'build up' and promote the growth and health of the entire Church.

options.a: enhance the beauty of the Church.
options.d: help the individual to be active

question: The importance of the parable of the unmerciful servant is that Christians should
solution: The Parable of the Unmerciful Servant (Matthew 18:21-35) teaches that because God has forgiven us an immeasurable debt, we must be faithful to forgive our fellow believers.

options.a: allow mercy to take precedence over justice
options.c: be faithful and not to betray their trust

question: Which of the following does not relate to the new life in Christ?
solution: The 'new life in Christ' is characterized by faith, grace, and baptism (the spiritual inward change), whereas 'circumcision' was the sign of the old Mosaic covenant (Galatians 5:6).

question: St. Paul's teaching on forgiveness primarily shows that________
solution: In Philemon 1, St. Paul teaches that true forgiveness and reconciliation are effectively facilitated when subordinates (like Onesimus) and superiors (like Philemon) treat each other as 'beloved brothers' in Christ.

options.a: Subordinates must regard their superiors and vice-versa for forgiveness to thrive
options.b: Genuine forgiveness only comes from God
options.c: Christians must not institute a 'Master-Servant' relationship contract
options.d: Philemon had unilaterally decided not to accept Onesimus back

question: St. John refers to Jesus’ miracles as ‘signs’ because they convey the following ideas except
solution: In John's Gospel, miracles are called 'signs' (Semeion) because they point to Jesus' divine identity. However, Jesus often rebuked those who followed Him only to see 'wonders' without faith (John 4:48), making D the exception.

options.a: acceptance of Jesus as son of God.
options.b: proofs of the kingdom of God.
options.c: faith in Jesus as the means to eternal life.

question: “My son your sins are forgiven”. These were the words of  Jesus to the
solution: In Mark 2:5, Jesus said 'My son, your sins are forgiven' to the paralytic who was lowered through the roof, thus linking his physical healing to spiritual restoration.

question: “This is the King of the Jews”This inscription on the cross was meant to show Jesus
solution: The inscription on the cross, 'This is the King of the Jews' (INRI), was meant as a 'charge' (the reason for His execution) in the eyes of the Roman authorities (Matthew 27, Luke 23).

question: In the Gospel according to St. Matthew, Jesus sent His disciples to_______
solution: In Matthew 15:24, Jesus initially states 'I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel' (the Jews), though His mission ultimately extended to all nations.

question: God displayed His supremacy when Ahab was king during 	the
solution: God's supremacy was dramatically displayed during the reign of King Ahab during the 'contest on Mount Carmel' (1 Kings 18), where fire fell from heaven to consume Elijah's sacrifice.

question: One of the following said: “the lion has roared who will not fear? The Lord has spoken, who can  but prophesy?”
solution: The prophet Amos (Amos 3:8) famously said 'The lion has roared; who will not fear? The Lord God has spoken; who can but prophesy?' to emphasize his unstoppable divine calling.

question: ‘Now I known that Lord has sent his angel to deliver me ……”who said this?
solution: In Acts 12:11, after being miraculously released from prison by an angel, Peter declared, 'Now I am sure that the Lord has sent his angel to deliver me.'

question: God charged man to 'be fruitful, and multiply, and .... the earth, and subdue it'. (1st creation story)
solution: In the first creation story (Genesis 1:28), God told the first humans to 'be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it,' calling them to stewardship.

question: The core of Saul's disobedience was that_____________
solution: The core of Saul's disobedience in 1 Samuel 15 was his failure to 'utterly destroy' everything related to the Amalekites as God had explicitly commanded, sparing King Agag and the best of the livestock.

options.b: He refused to carry out God's assignment on eliminating the Amalekites
options.c: He didn't perform the task of smiting Amalekites to the letter
options.d: He preserved the booty for himself

question: After his conversion, Paul was introduced to the disciples in Jerusalem by
solution: After his radical conversion, Paul was initially feared by the apostles in Jerusalem until 'Barnabas took him and brought him to the apostles' (Acts 9:27), vouching for his authenticity.

question: Who was authorized by God to go and liberate the Israelites from the Egyptian bondage?
solution: In the book of Exodus (Exodus 3), God appeared to Moses in a burning bush and authorized him to go before Pharaoh to 'liberate the Israelites from Egyptian bondage.'

question: According to Romans, when civil authorities punish evil doers, they do so as the
solution: In Romans 13:1-7, Paul teaches that civil authorities are 'ministers of God' and serve as 'agents of God's wrath' against those who do evil, thus maintaining order.

options.c: agents of the darkness of this world

question: Jesus’ call of the first four disciples to catch men rather than fish implies that they were to
solution: When Jesus called His first four disciples, He told them he would make them 'fishers of men' (Mark 1), implying they were to 'propagate the teachings of Jesus' and win souls for the kingdom.

question: The person who discovered the book of law in the sanctuary during the reign of Josiah was
solution: During a temple renovation in the reign of King Josiah, Hilkiah the high priest 'found the book of the law' in the house of the Lord (2 Kings 22:8), triggering a massive religious reform.

question: The land, the sea and vegetation were created on the
solution: In the first creation story (Genesis 1:9-13), God separated the waters and let dry land appear, created the seas, and brought forth vegetation on the 'third day'.

question: According to Paul in Galatians, law ceased to be our custodian as soon as
solution: In Galatians 3:24-26, Paul explains that the Law was our 'custodian' until Christ came, after which we were 'adopted as children' through faith and are now under grace.

options.a: it was made available to us.
options.b: Christ adopted us as his children.
options.c: we adopted the faith of Abraham.
options.d: we stopped worshipping the elemental spirits

question: Which of the following was the first directive given to man after He had created him?
solution: The first directive given to man after creation (Genesis 1:28) was to 'be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth and subdue it.'

options.a: Be fruitful and multiply, fill the earth and subdue
options.b: Go ye into the world and exercise full authority over
options.c: Be you kind to all creatures of the earth
options.d: Behold I have given you every plant yielding seed

question: The man of God whose word was ignored by Kings Ahab and Jehoshaphat was
solution: Micaiah the son of Imlah was the prophet whose unpopular word was ignored by King Ahab of Israel and King Jehoshaphat of Judah before their ill-fated battle at Ramoth-gilead (1 Kings 22).

question: “Lord, if you will, you can make me clean.” The statement above was made by the
solution: In several Gospel accounts (e.g., Matthew 8:2), it was a 'leper' who approached Jesus and said, 'Lord, if you will, you can make me clean,' expressing his faith in Jesus' power.

question: The Lord protected the Israelites with all but one of the following when the Egyptians chased them
solution: During the Exodus, God protected the Israelites with a 'pillar of cloud' by day and a 'pillar of fire' (not darkness) by night, as well as His angel, to guide and guard them (Exodus 14), making D ('pillar of storm') the correct exception.

question: Instead of being unnecessarily anxious about life and the affairs of this world, Jesus taught us to
solution: In the Sermon on the Mount (Matthew 6:25-34), Jesus taught His followers that instead of being anxious about worldly needs like food and clothing, they should 'seek first the kingdom of God and its righteousness.'

options.a: relax our minds and wait for the manna from above
options.b: seek first the kingdom of God and its righteousness
options.c: seek water baptism and qualify for divine healing
options.d: repent of our sins and preach the gospel.

question: The model example in St. Paul teaching on humility is _______
solution: In his letter to the Philippians (Chapter 2), Paul uses Jesus Christ as the absolute model and example of humility, describing how He 'emptied himself' and took the form of a servant (Phil 2:5-8).

question: After Prophet Ezekiel had eaten what he was offered, he was sent to speak to
solution: After Ezekiel performed the symbolic act of 'eating the scroll' offered by God (Ezekiel 3:1-4), he was commissioned to go and speak God’s word 'to the house of Israel.'

question: Israel went into Assyrain captivity in
solution: The northern kingdom of Israel was conquered and led into Assyrian captivity in 722 BC (2 Kings 17), following the fall of Samaria to the Assyrian Empire.

question: Which of the following is the result of Christian baptism?
solution: In Romans 6:4, Paul explains that one result of Christian baptism is to be buried with Christ so that 'just as Christ was raised from the dead... we too might walk in newness of life.'

options.b: Walking in the newness of life
options.d: Yielding of one’s life to prayer.

question: God visited the children of Israel on Mount Sinai during the giving of  the commandments through
solution: When God gave the Ten Commandments on Mount Sinai (Exodus 19-20), His presence was accompanied by 'smoke and fire', lightning, and thunder, as the cloud descended upon the mountain.

question: The minority report of the twelve spies was presented by
solution: The minority report of the twelve spies (Numbers 13-14) was presented by 'Caleb and Joshua', who alone among the twelve believed that Israel could conquer the Promised Land with God's help.

question: According to Isaiah, God sent him to
solution: In the 'Servant Songs' (e.g., Isaiah 61), the prophet declares that God has sent him to 'proclaim liberty to the captives and the opening of the prison to those who are bound.'

question: The sons of Samuel misused their privileged positions by
solution: The sons of Samuel (Joel and Abijah) misused their positions as judges by turning aside after gain, 'taking bribes and perverting justice' (1 Samuel 8:3), leading to the people's demand for a king.

options.a: not allowing the fats of animals to burn first to God
options.b: sleeping with women who came to sacrifice

question: In their dealings with God, Abraham and Sarah learnt the following lessons except that God is
solution: In their journey with God, Abraham and Sarah learned that God is faithful, all-powerful, and 'blesses the righteous.' However, they also learned that God often works in His own timing, making C a point of lessons learned (nothing is too hard).

options.c: all powerful-nothing is too hard for Him.
options.d: not in a hurry to carry out His promises.

question: When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the gospel, they sent to them
solution: Acts 8:14 records that when the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent 'Peter and John' to pray for them to receive the Holy Spirit.

question: Solomon made the most unwise decision by engaging in
solution: Solomon's most unwise decision (1 Kings 11) was 'marrying foreign women' from nations God had forbidden, who eventually turned his heart away from Yahweh toward false gods.

question: A good example of parental responsibility was demonstrated by
solution: In the context of parental responsibility, King Asa (1 Kings 15, 2 Chronicles 14-16) is often cited as a more righteous and responsible king than figures like Eli or Rehoboam.

question: During the Ammonite war, Uriah disobeyed King David by refusing to go to his house to sleep because
solution: During the Ammonite war, Uriah the Hittite refused to go home to sleep with his wife out of solidarity with his fellow soldiers and the Ark which were in tents, citing it as 'unpatriotic to break his military vow' (2 Samuel 11).

options.a: it was wrong to go home drunk
options.b: his wife would regard him as a coward
options.c: it would be unpatriotic to break military vow
options.d: he suspected that the king had lost interest in the battle

question: According to James, treatment of brethren at fellowship should not be based on
solution: In James 2:1-4, the apostle teaches that partiality or discrimination toward brethren should not be based on 'personal appearance' or clothing (the rich vs the poor at fellowship).

question: The prayer of Jesus at Gethsemane shows that
solution: The 'agony' in Gethsemane (Luke 22:44), where Jesus' sweat became like great drops of blood, shows that He was in total 'severe agony' as He submitted His will to the Father.

options.c: He needed the support of His disciples

question: In the Epistle to Galatians, Christians are children of God through
solution: Paul clearly teaches in Galatians 3:26 that 'in Christ Jesus you are all children of God, through faith.'

question: The most important aspect of the religious reforms of King Josiah was the
solution: While King Josiah's reign saw temple cleaning and a covenant renewal, the catalyst for the entire reform was the 'discovery of the book of the law' in the house of the Lord (2 Kings 22).

options.c: discovery of the tomb of the man of God
options.d: discovery of the book of the law

question: The name Ichabod means
solution: The name 'Ichabod' (1 Samuel 4:21) was given to the son of Phinehas by his dying mother, meaning 'The glory has departed from Israel,' as the Ark of God had been captured.

options.c: I have asked him from the Lord
options.d: the glory has departed from Israel.

question: Why was Saul asked to destroy the Amalekites?
solution: God commanded Saul to destroy the Amalekites because they had opposed and attacked the Israelites when they were vulnerable after leaving Egypt (Exodus 17:8-16, 1 Samuel 15:2).

options.b: They opposed the Israelites when they left Egypt
options.c: They did not assist the Israelites
options.d: God did not like the Amalekites

question: According to Jeremiah, the people of Israel were in exile because of their
solution: Jeremiah and other prophets taught that Israel's exile was a direct result of their rebellion against God's covenant, though the physical cause of the 586 BC exile was their rebellion against King Nebuchadnezzar (2 Kings 24-25).

question: Paul argued that Jesus died for us while we were yet sinners in order to
solution: In Romans 5:8, Paul argues that 'God shows his love for us in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us,' highlighting the unconditional nature of divine grace.

options.a: prove that he was not an ordinary man
options.b: show Gods’ love for sinful man
options.c: warn us to desist from sin
options.d: give us power to possess the earth

question: Who was the secretary employed by Jeremiah to write his prophecy?
solution: Baruch the son of Neriah was the faithful secretary and friend of the prophet Jeremiah, who wrote down Jeremiah's prophecies at his dictation (Jeremiah 36).

question: St Paul's epistle to the Philippians on "Christian Giving' indicated his reception of gifts from them through________
solution: In Philippians 4:18, Paul acknowledges that he received the financial gifts and support sent by the church in Philippi through their messenger, Epaphroditus.

question: The new covenant between God and the Israelites is centered on
solution: The 'New Covenant' promised in Jeremiah 31:31-34 is centered on 'individual responsibility' and the Law being written on the hearts of men, rather than on tablets of stone.

question: During the period of three and a half years of drought in Israel, the man who hid one hundred prophets of God in a cave was
solution: Obadiah, the steward for King Ahab, was a devout believer who hid 100 prophets of Yahweh in two caves and fed them during the severe drought caused by Ahab's idolatry (1 Kings 18:4).

question: Eutychus fell from the third storey to the ground when Paul  prolonged his sermon at
solution: In Troas (Acts 20:9-12), a young man named Eutychus fell from a third-story window after falling asleep during Paul's prolonged midnight sermon, but Paul miraculously restored him to life.

question: Jesus charged Christians to express love just as________
solution: Jesus gave the 'New Commandment' (John 13:34) for His disciples to 'love one another just as I have loved you,' pointing to His selfless life and sacrificial death as the ultimate model.

options.a: He taught them the new commandment
options.b: They would do with their neighbours
options.c: He laid his life for the sake of mankind

question: “To obey is better than sacrifice” came from
solution: The quote 'to obey is better than sacrifice' was spoken by the prophet Samuel to King Saul after Saul failed to follow God's instructions regarding the Amalekites (1 Samuel 15:22).

question: The name "Jezreel" is a representation of ______
solution: In the book of Hosea, the name 'Jezreel' (Hosea 1:4) represented the coming punishment and judgment upon the royal house of Israel for the massacre committed at Jezreel.

options.b: Victory upon the enemies of Israel
options.c: The Punishment to come unto the royal house
options.d: The broken covenant between God and Israel

question: “Talitha cumi” means
solution: The Aramaic expression 'Talitha cumi' (Mark 5:41) spoken by Jesus to Jairus' daughter literally means 'Little girl, I say to you, arise.'

options.b: Little girl, I say to you, arise

question: Peter healed Aeneas who had been bedridden for ____ years?
solution: Acts 9:33-34 records that Peter healed a man named Aeneas in Lydda, who had been paralyzed and bedridden for 'eight years.'

question: Who was the first king of Israel?
solution: Saul (the son of Kish) was the first king of Israel, anointed by Samuel at God's direction after the people demanded a king to lead them (1 Samuel 9-10).

question: The action that portrayed Moses as a leader of the Israelites was his
solution: Moses' early leadership potential was shown in his active (though impulsive) 'killing of an Egyptian' who was beating a Hebrew kinsman, showing his solidarity with his people (Exodus 2:11-12).

options.a: request to Pharaoh to spare Israelite’s sons
options.c: killing of an Egyptian who fought with a Hebrew
options.d: protection of the daughter of Jethro

question: The expression ‘dry bones’ in Ezekiel refers to
solution: The vision of the 'dry bones' in Ezekiel 37 refers to 'the whole house of Israel', symbolizing their spiritual death in exile and God's promise of future national restoration.

question: The commander of David’s army was
solution: Joab (the son of Zeruiah) was the supreme commander of David’s army and a key figure in David's military campaigns, though he often acted on his own initiative (2 Samuel 2).

question: Paul compares the manifestations of spiritual gifts in individual members of the Church to the
solution: In 1 Corinthians 12, Paul uses the 'unity of the human body' as a metaphor to explain how spiritual gifts should function—each part being different but necessary for the whole Church.

options.b: rights of the individual members of the Church
options.c: rights of the different parts of the body

question: The person who the Bible calls the son of consolation is
solution: In Acts 4:36, Joseph, a Levite from Cyprus, is introduced as 'Barnabas', a name given to him by the apostles which means 'son of consolation' (or encouragement).

question: Whoever drinks the water Jesus gives will never thirst? What is this water?
solution: In John 4 (the woman at the well), Jesus speaks of 'living water' that satisfies forever, which is interpreted as 'the spirit of God' and the eternal life it brings.

question: Before their first encounter with Jesus, Peter and Andrew were
solution: Before their encounter with Jesus at the Sea of Galilee, Simon Peter and his brother Andrew were 'fishermen' by trade, who were then called to become fishers of men (Matthew 4:18-20).

question: Paul considered himself unfit to be called an apostle because he
solution: In 1 Corinthians 15:9, Paul humility stated that he was 'the least of the apostles, unfit to be called an apostle' because he had once persecuted the church of God.

options.c: worked hard to attain his position
options.d: was the least among the believers.

question: In which of the following ways did the Philippians share in Paul’s suffering while in prison?
solution: In his letter to the Philippians (Philippians 4:14-18), Paul acknowledged that they shared in his' suffering by 'sending him relief materials' and financial support through Epaphroditus.

options.a: Appealing for the release of Paul
options.d: Remaining in partnership with him in receiving

question: What was the second temptation of Jesus according to the Gospel according to Luke?
solution: In the Gospel of Luke (4:1-13), the 'second temptation' of Jesus is the devil's offer of worldy power in exchange for Jesus 'worshipping him.'

options.a: The Devil wanted Jesus to worship him
options.b: The Devil wanted Jesus to deny God
options.c: The Devil wanted Jesus to jump from the pinnacle of the Temple
options.d: The devil wanted Jesus to turn stones to bread

question: One of the most significant lessons from the emergence of Deborah as a leader is that_________
solution: Deborah’s emergence as a judge and leader (Judges 4) was a powerful lesson that 'gender difference isn't a limitation to leadership' in God's eyes, even in an era of patriarchal norms.

options.a: She not only prophesied but also advised Israel
options.b: Gender difference isn't a limitation to leadership
options.c: Honesty and Integrity are key to leadership
options.d: One needs to summon courage as a leader to solve problems

question: Paul informed the Galatians that the gospel he preached came through (Gal. 3:23-29)
solution: Paul told the Galatians (Galatians 1:11-12) that the gospel he preached was 'not a human message' but was received through 'a direct revelation of Jesus Christ.'

question: During the rebuilding of the temple, Zerubbabel rejected the offer of help from the
solution: During the rebuilding of the temple (Ezra 4:1-3), Zerubbabel and the leaders of Israel 'rejected the offer of help' from the people of Samaria, who were not considered true Israelites.

options.d: governors of the provinces beyond the rivers.

question: Who according to James blaspheme the honourable name by which Christians are called
solution: In James 2:6-7, the apostle warns that it is often 'rich people' who 'blaspheme the honourable name' of Christ by exploiting and discriminating against those with less means.

question: ‘Do not destroy him; for who can stretch out his hand on the LORD’S anointed, and be guiltless?’ In the statement above, David was acknowledging God’s role in
solution: In 1 Samuel 26:9, David stayed Abishai's hand from killing Saul, the 'Lord's anointed,' thus acknowledging God's role in 'Saul's leadership' and placement as king.

question: On the day of Ascension, Jesus promised his disciples
solution: In Acts 1:8, on the day of His ascension, Jesus promised His disciples that they would 'receive power when the Holy Ghost has come upon you' to be His witnesses in the world.

options.a: peace in the kingdom of Go

question: To St Paul, Christians can prepare for the second coming of Christ by______
solution: In 1 Thessalonians 5, Paul teaches that Christians should prepare for the Lord's return by 'being prayerful and watchful' and living with sober-mindedness (righteous living).

options.b: Spreading the message of Christ's coming far and wide

question: “…who is the Lord that I should heed his voice and let Israel go”? After this statement, Pharaoh
solution: Pharaoh's defiant response (Exodus 5:2) to Moses and Aaron's first visit was to 'ask the Israelites to fetch their own straws' while still maintaining the same brick production quota.

options.a: engaged Israel into forced labour with task masters.
options.b: ordered midwives to kill all males born to Israelites.
options.c: asked Israelites to fetch their own straws to produce bricks.
options.d: decreed that Israelites’ male babies be drowned in the Nile.

question: In his second letter to the Christians in Thessalonians, Paul admonished them to have nothing to do with anyone who refused to obey what he wrote in the letter. What did Paul think would happen to the person?
solution: In 2 Thessalonians 3:14, Paul instructs the church to 'note well' whoever is idle and disobedient so that they might 'feel ashamed' and turn from their ways.

question: “Don’t be afraid, henceforth you will be catching men”. “Catching men” means capturing men
solution: Jesus' call to his disciples that they would be 'catching men' (Luke 5:10) was a metaphor for 'bringing people into the new faith' and the kingdom of God through the gospel.

question: According to Luke, the multitude that followed Jesus during the triumphal entry rejoiced and praised God with a loud voice because (Luke 19:37-39)
solution: In Luke 19:37-38, the multitude praised God with a loud voice during the triumphal entry 'for all the mighty works that they had seen' Jesus perform.

options.a: they realized that Jesus was a prophet
options.b: of the mighty works He had done
options.c: He was riding on a colt in humility
options.d: they believed He was their Messiah

question: During the election to replace Judas among the twelve, Peter quoted from the book of
solution: In Acts 1:20, Peter quoted from the book of 'Psalms' (Psalms 69 and 109) to justify the selection of a replacement for Judas among the twelve.

question: David bitterly mourned when Abner was slain by
solution: In 2 Samuel 3:27-33, David bitumenously mourned the death of Abner, who was murdered by 'Joab' out of revenge for the death of Joab's brother, Asahel.

question: In Corinthians, Paul stated that he was unfit to be called an apostle because he
solution: In 1 Corinthians 15:9, Paul again emphasizes his unworthiness for an apostolic call because he 'persecuted the church of God' before his Damascus road experience.

options.c: was not one of the twelve.

question: Naaman’s leprosy was transferred to Gehazi be¬cause Gehazi was
solution: Naaman's leprosy was transferred to Gehazi (2 Kings 5:20-27) because Gehazi was 'covetous' and deceptively took gifts from Naaman that Elisha had already refused.

question: The name Moses means
solution: According to Exodus 2:10, Pharaoh's daughter named the child 'Moses' (Mosheh) because she had 'drawn him out of the water' of the Nile.

question: In Philippians, Paul taught that Jesus ‘humility and death on the cross earned Him
solution: In Philippians 2:9, Paul writes that because of Jesus' humility and obedience, God 'highly exalted him' and bestowed on Him the name that is above every name, thus earning Him 'exaltation as Lord.'

question: Who among the seven deacons was an evangelist who had four daughters who prophesied?
solution: Philip the evangelist (Acts 8 and 21:8) was one of the original seven deacons and is specifically mentioned as having 'four unmarried daughters who prophesied.'

question: Daniel was delivered from the lion’s den because
solution: Daniel was delivered from the lion’s den (Daniel 6:23) because he trusted in God and no kind of harm was found on him because he 'had believed in his God.'

options.a: of his faith in God’s power.
options.c: he was able to charm the lions.
options.d: the lions were kind to him.

question: According to the Letter to the Hebrews, why should Christians lay aside every weight and sin which clings so closely?
solution: In Hebrews 12:1, the author urges Christians to 'lay aside every weight and sin' because they are 'surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses' (the examples of faith from the past).

options.b: Christian can easily be influenced by sinners
options.c: Christians are surrounded by so great a cloud of witness
options.d: No sinner will inherit the kingdom of God

question: Peter’s tour of Lydda led to the
solution: Acts 9:35 records that after Peter healed Aeneas in Lydda, 'all the residents of Lydda and Sharon saw him, and they turned to the Lord.' Sharon was the coastal plain region where they lived.

options.a: development of the church in Caesarca
options.b: conversion of the residents of sharon
options.c: conversion of Cornelius and his household
options.d: healing of Dorcas and her family

question: “Tell people, His disciples came by night and stole him away while we were asleep...” The intention of the speaker in the statement above was to
solution: In Matthew 28:11-15, the chief priests gave money to the soldiers so they would lie and say the disciples 'stole Jesus' body while we were asleep' to specifically 'deny the resurrection story.'

options.b: absolve the soldiers from all blame

question: God instructed Saul to utterly destroy Amalek because_____
solution: God commanded Saul to 'utterly destroy' the Amalekites (1 Samuel 15:2) because they had 'opposed the Israelites on the way' when they were exiting Egypt.

options.a: Its people encouraged Israel to disobey God
options.b: He abhors Idolatory and all forms of unrighteousness
options.c: Samuel had prayed for its destruction
options.d: Its people opposed the Israelites on the way, after departing Egypt

question: Moses was nursed by
solution: According to Exodus 2:7-9, Pharaoh's daughter allowed a Hebrew woman (who was actually 'Moses' own mother') to nurse and care for him after he was found in the Nile.

question: Which prophet encouraged Jeroboam I to  revolt at the initial stage?
solution: At the beginning of the divided kingdom (1 Kings 11:29-31), 'Ahijah the Shilonite' (not Joel) was the prophet who encouraged Jeroboam I of the coming revolt and division.

question: By removing his sandals before the burning bush, Moses was showing a sense of
solution: By removing his sandals at the burning bush (Exodus 3:5), Moses was showing a deep sense of 'reverence and submissiveness' before the presence of the Holy God.

question: The descent of the Holy Spirit on the day of  Pentecost was prophesied by
solution: The descent of the Holy Spirit on Pentecost (Acts 2:16-17) was explained by Peter as the fulfillment of a prophecy from the prophet 'Joel' (Joel 2:28-32).

question: Discipline requires self- denial and complete
solution: In his epistles and teachings, Paul and other apostles emphasize that true Christian 'discipline' and discipleship in the kingdom of God require both 'self-denial' and 'complete commitment.'

question: "Do not be afraid; for I know what you seek, Jesus who was crucified..." This statement was made__________
solution: Matthew 28:5-7 records that an angel of the Lord appeared and spoke the words 'Do not be afraid' to 'Mary Magdalene and the other Mary' (Mary the mother of James) at the empty tomb.

options.b: to Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James
options.c: to Mary Salome, Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James

question: Angel Gabriel told Zachariah that he would become dumb until John was born because he
solution: In Luke 1:20, the angel Gabriel told Zechariah that he would be silent (dumb) until John's birth because he 'did not believe the angel's words' regarding his wife's future pregnancy.

question: What did Gideon call the altar which he built to the LORD?
solution: In Judges 6:24, after God's angel appeared to him, Gideon built an altar and called it 'Jehovah-Shalom,' which translated into English means 'The LORD is peace.'

question: Paul said he was not fit to be called an apostle because he
solution: In 1 Corinthians 15:9, Paul again states his sense of unfitness as an apostle due to his past actions where he 'persecuted the early church' of God.

options.a: was not called like the others
options.d: was only a distributor of letters

question: ‘Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Jona ! For flesh and blood has not revealed this to you...’ As exemplified by the statement above, for believers to be able to identify with Jesus, they must
solution: Jesus' statement to Peter (Matthew 16:17) that 'flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but my Father in heaven' shows that identifying Jesus as the Christ requires one to 'be led by the Spirit.'

question: The king who captured and drove the Israelites into exile during the reign of Jehoiachin was
solution: In 2 Kings 24:10-16, King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon besieged and captured Jerusalem, eventually deporting King Jehoiachin and thousands of people into exile.

question: What is meant by the Christian doctrine that God is Trinity?
solution: The Christian doctrine of the 'Trinity' states that there is one God who exists eternally in 'three persons' (the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit) who are one in essence.

options.a: the idea started in Trinidad and Tobago
options.b: God is three persons in one

question: Josiah’s reign was popular because of his
solution: King Josiah’s reign in Judah (2 Kings 22-23) was extremely popular and influential primarily because of his extensive and radical 'religious reforms' based on the newly discovered Book of the Law.

question: According to Joseph’s interpretation to Pharaoh’s dreams;
solution: In Genesis 41, Joseph interpreted Pharaoh's dreams as representing семь years of great abundance followed by семь years of severe 'famine' that would consume all the previous plenty.

question: Eve was to have pains at childbirth because she
solution: In Genesis 3:16, God told Eve that as a result of her 'eating of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil', she would experience greatly multiplied pain in childbirth.

options.c: ate of the tree of life.
options.d: ate of the tree of good and evil.

question: The person who was invited to nurse Moses was
solution: In Exodus 2:7-9, Moses' sister Miriam arranged for 'Jochebed' (their own mother) to be hired and paid by Pharaoh's daughter to nurse the infant Moses.

question: Saul was converted on his journey to
solution: Acts 9 records that the radical conversion of Saul of Tarsus occurred while he was on his 'journey to Damascus' to arrest followers of the Way.

question: Where did Paul cure a slave girl who had a spirit of divination
solution: In Acts 16:16-18, during his second missionary journey, Paul was in 'Philippi' when he cast out a spirit of divination from a slave girl who was being exploited for profit.

question: The baptism of Jesus by John in River Jordan was to
solution: In Matthew 3:15, Jesus told John the Baptist that His baptism was necessary 'to fulfill all righteousness', identifying with humanity and formally starting His public ministry.

options.a: convince others that John was His forerunner
options.b: ensure that proper baptism must be in a river

question: At the coming of the Lord, unbelievers will
solution: In 2 Thessalonians 1:8-9, Paul teaches that at the coming of the Lord, 'unbelievers' who do not know God or obey the gospel will suffer the penalty of eternal 'condemnation'.

question: “How is it that you, a Jew, ask a drink of me, a woman of 	Samaria?” The speaker was surprised because
solution: In John 4:9, the Samaritan woman was surprised that Jesus, a Jew, would ask her for water because 'Jews have no dealings with Samaritans' due to centuries of religious and ethnic rivalry.

options.a: Jesus was God and not human
options.b: the Jews could only buy water from Samaria
options.c: the Jews had no dealings with Samaria
options.d: it was unlawful for a man to receive water from a woman

question: In the early Church, believer devoted themselves to the
solution: Acts 2:42-46 describes how the early believers in Jerusalem devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching, fellowship, and the 'breaking of bread' (communion) from house to house.

options.c: breaking of bread in the Temple

question: Which of the following prophets taught that Yahweh was merciful?
solution: The prophet Hosea (Hosea 6:6) is famously associated with the message that Yahweh is 'merciful' and desires steadfast love and mercy rather than mere ritual sacrifice.

question: ‘Deal gently for my sake with the young man Absalom.’ David’s statement above exhibited his
solution: Before the battle against Absalom's rebels (2 Samuel 18:5), King David's command to 'deal gently with the young man Absalom' highlighted his 'indulgent and persistent love' for his rebellious son.

question: Which of the following places was not visited by Paul during his first missionary journey?
solution: During his first missionary journey (Acts 13-14), Paul and Barnabas visited Cyprus (Salamis and Paphos) and parts of Galatia. Athens was visited later during the second journey.

question: Who baptized the Ethiopian Eunuch?
solution: Acts 8:26-39 records that 'Philip' the evangelist explained the scriptures and 'baptized the Ethiopian eunuch' on the desert road from Jerusalem to Gaza.

question: In order that Benjamin might be brought to Egypt, Joseph
solution: In Genesis 42:24, as part of his strategy to test his brothers and bring Benjamin to Egypt, Joseph took 'Simeon' (Simon) from them and had him bound before their eyes as a hostage.

question: Israel went into Assyrain captivity in
solution: The northern kingdom of Israel was conquered and led into Assyrian captivity in 722 BC (2 Kings 17), following the fall of Samaria to the Assyrian King Shalmaneser V.

question: When Abner wanted to transfer his allegiance to David, the latter gave him the condition to
solution: When Abner offered to bring all Israel over to David (2 Samuel 3:13), David stipulated that he would only see Abner's face if he first 'restored Michal' (Saul's daughter and David's first wife) to him.

options.b: convince all Israel to come over to David.
options.c: restore his wife Michal to him.

question: Solomon showed lack of wisdom in
solution: Despite his great gift of wisdom (1 Kings 11:1-4), King Solomon 'showed a lack of wisdom' by 'marrying foreign wives' who eventually led his heart toward idolatry.

options.b: his bilateral agreement with other nations.

question: “It is not the sound of shouting for victory, or the sound of the cry of defeat, but the sound of singing that I hear” When was this statement made?
solution: In Exodus 32:18, Joshua mistakenly thought he heard the sound of war, but Moses correctly identified the 'sound of singing' and revelry during the 'strange worship' of the Golden Calf.

options.a: at the consecration of the Israelites
options.b: at the crossing of the Red Sea
options.d: in a strange worship of a god

question: The core of Saul's disobedience was that___________
solution: Saul's core disobedience in 1 Samuel 15 was that 'he didn't perform the task to the letter', sparing King Agag and the best of the livestock despite God's command for total destruction.

options.a: He didn't perform the task of smiting the Amalekites to the letter
options.b: He preserved the booty for himself
options.d: He refused to carry out God's assignment on eliminating the Amalekites

question: “Shall l pursue after this band? Shall l overtake them?” David’s dependence on God for direction is depicted in the statement above when
solution: 1 Samuel 30 records that David inquired of the Lord, 'Shall I pursue after this band?' after the 'Amalekites had raided and burned Ziklag' and taken his wives and children captive.

options.d: he was to go against Goliath

question: In one of the resurrection appearances Jesus asked his disciples, whether they had anything to eat as he
solution: In post-resurrection accounts (e.g., Luke 24:41-43), Jesus asked for food (broiled fish and honeycomb) specifically to 'prove to His disciples that He was not a ghost' but had a physical, resurrected body.

options.a: was hungry after three days in the grave without food
options.b: suspected that the disciples stock may have finished
options.c: wanted to prove to them that he was not a ghost.
options.d: wanted to replenish the food stuff.

question: On the day of Ascension, Jesus promised his disciples
solution: In Acts 1:8, on the day of His ascension into heaven, Jesus promised His disciples that they would 'receive power from the Holy Ghost' to be His witnesses in Jerusalem and all the world.

options.a: peace in the kingdom of God.

question: The triumphal entry was in fulfilment of the prophecy of_______
solution: The triumphal entry of Jesus into Jerusalem on a colt (Matthew 21:4-5) was in direct 'fulfilment of the prophecy of Zechariah' (Zechariah 9:9) regarding the humble King.

question: The workmen who repaired the house of the LORD during Josiah’s religious reforms were not made to account for the money given them because they
solution: 2 Kings 22:7 records that the 'honest workmen' who repaired the Temple during King Josiah's reforms were not required to give an account of the money because they 'dealt faithfully'.

options.a: did not receive the money directly

question: Eli’s inability to discipline his sons led to all the following except
solution: Eli's failure to discipline his corrupt sons led to God's curse and the 'cancellation of permanent priesthood' from his lineage, but it did not 'extend his life span'—it shortened it (1 Samuel 2-4).

options.b: the extension of his life span
options.c: the cancellation of priesthood from his lineage

question: ‘What is this that you have done!”  The people in the ship asked Jonah the question above because
solution: In Jonah 1, the mariners asked Jonah, 'What is this that you have done!' because they 'realized that he was fleeing from the presence of the LORD' and that God had sent the storm for him.

options.a: he was fast asleep in the ship
options.b: they knew he was fleeing from the LORD
options.c: he lied to them about his presence in the ship
options.d: they realized that he had made attempts to commit suicide.

question: ‘‘... and many will rejoice at his birth; for he will be great before the Lord...” In the statement above, the angel made this pronouncement because of
solution: Luke 1:13-17 records the angel Gabriel's pronouncement that John the Baptist would be great before the Lord, specifically 'in response to Zachariah's fervent and persistent prayers' (despite his initial disbelief).

options.a: Elizabeth’s disbelief because of her bareness

question: According to Peter, a Christian should exercise his freedom by
solution: In 1 Peter 2:15-16, the apostle teaches that Christians should express their freedom by 'doing what is right' and using their liberty for God’s service, not as a pretext for evil.

options.b: claiming his freedom at all cost.
options.d: using it as a pretext for evil.

question: According to the Mosaic law, the only authority to declare a leper healed was
solution: Under the Mosaic law (Leviticus 13-14), the formal authority and 'only specialized authority' to 'declare a leper clean and healed' was 'the priest' after a physical inspection.

question: Paul was stoned and dragged out of the city by
solution: Acts 14:19 records that 'Jews from Antioch and Iconium' came to Lystra, won over the crowds, and 'stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city' thinking he was dead.

options.b: enemies of the healed lame man

question: In his teaching on Spiritual gifts in Corinthians, Paul said that all gifts are inspired by
solution: In 1 Corinthians 12, Paul teaches that the variety of spiritual gifts, services, and activities are all 'inspired for use through the same Spirit' for the benefit of the body of Christ.

question: In the case before Solomon over the deal and the living, child the mother of the dead child supported.
solution: In the famous judgment of Solomon (1 Kings 3:26), the lying mother (of the dead child) supported 'killing the living child' (splitting it in two) as a way to ensure neither woman could have him.

options.a: Giving the living child to her opponent
options.c: joint ownership of the living child
options.d: king Solomon having the living child

question: In the parable of the sower, the seed that fell among the thorns symbolized the one who hears the word but
solution: In the Parable of the Sower (Matthew 13:22, Mark 4:19), the seed falling among thorns represents the one who hears the word, but 'the cares of the world and delight in riches choke it.'

options.d: the cares of the world choke it.

question: ________ was the man James used as example of how the effective prayer of the righteous man works
solution: In James 5:17-18, the apostle uses the 'prophet Elijah' as a model example of how the 'effective and fervent prayer of a righteous man' can shut and open the heavens for rain.

question: Who is an atheist?
solution: An 'atheist' is formally defined as 'someone who rejects completely the idea of any divine being or supernatural god' as a reality or possibility.

options.a: someone who does believe in God
options.b: someone who rejects completely the idea of a divine being
options.c: someone who believes that it is impossible to know if there is God or not

question: Peter admonished Christians to be sober and watchful in order to avoid
solution: In 1 Peter 5:8, the apostle warns Christians to 'be sober and watchful' because their adversary, the devil, 'prowls around like a roaring lion seeking someone to devour.'

question: Which prophet cried thus “woe is me” for lost, I am a man of unclean lips
solution: In his prophetic call vision (Isaiah 6:5), 'Isaiah' cried out 'Woe is me! For I am lost; for I am a man of unclean lips' after seeing the glory of the Lord in the Temple.

question: Paul in Thessalonian said the signs for the Second Coming of Christ would start with
solution: In 2 Thessalonians 2:3, Paul states that the second coming of Christ cannot occur until the 'rebellion' (Apostasy) comes first and the 'man of lawlessness' is revealed.

question: Why did Pilate send Jesus to Herod during the trial?
solution: Under Roman law (Luke 23:7), Pilate initially sent Jesus to Herod Antipas for trial because Jesus was a Galilean and was thus 'under the jurisdiction of Herod', who was in Jerusalem.

options.a: Jesus, as a Galilean was under Herod’s jurisdiction
options.b: Pilate had no power to pass the death sentence
options.c: Herod’s court was the last point before crucifixion
options.d: Herod had longed to see Jesus perform miracles

question: What specifically makes Samson a unique prophet of God?
solution: Samson (Judges 13:3-5) was unique from birth because he was a 'Nazirite' (consecrated to God), which involved specific vows like never cutting his hair or drinking wine.

options.c: He killed many at his death
options.d: He spoke with power in riddles

question: Where did Paul cure a slave girl who had a spirit of divination?
solution: In Acts 16:16-18, during Paul's ministry in 'Philippi,' he encountered a slave girl who was possessed by a spirit of divination (Pythian spirit) which he cast out in Jesus' name.

question: Jesus told the parable of the lost sheep in order to illustrate that
solution: Jesus told the Parable of the Lost Sheep (Luke 15) specifically to illustrate that 'there is more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents' than over ninety-nine righteous persons.

options.a: every believer ought to rejoice with those who rejoice.
options.b: no person can hide from God
options.d: there is joy in heaven over a sinner who repents

question: According to John’s Gospel, Lazarus was from
solution: In John 11:1, Lazarus (whom Jesus raised from the dead) was a native of 'Bethany', the village of his sisters Mary and Martha, located near Jerusalem.

question: By saying that no human being will be justified in the sight of God by works: Paul meant that
solution: In Romans 3:20, Paul explains that 'no human being will be justified in his sight by works of the law', because the law's primary function in the human flesh was providing the knowledge of sin.

options.a: the mosaic law was useless in ensuring justification for man.
options.b: the law had been weakened in human flesh.
options.c: knowledge of sin came through the law
options.d: Moses who gave the law was not justified by the law.

question: When Rhoda reported that Peter was at the door, the inmates retorted that
solution: Acts 12:13-15 records that when the servant girl Rhoda reported that Peter was miraculously at the door, the believers inside initially retorted 'You are mad!', thinking it was his angel.

question: Why did Paul wish to retain Onesimus?
solution: In his letter to Philemon, Paul wished to keep the runaway slave Onesimus with him 'to serve and assist him during his imprisonment' for the gospel (Philemon 1:13).

options.c: Philemon had earnestly requested that from Paul
options.d: To serve him during his imprisonment

question: “Give meat for the priest to roast for he will not accept boiled meat from you, but raw”This request was wrong because in a peace offering
solution: The request by Eli's sons (Hophni and Phinehas) for raw meat (1 Samuel 2:15-17) was a grievous sin because God's law required the 'fat to be burned to the Lord' BEFORE the priest's share was taken.

options.b: juicy meat went to the worshippers
options.c: the fat was burnt before  any distribution
options.d: priests were entitled to the right thigh and breast

question: In St. Luke’s record, Jesus specially sent the seventy disciples on mission to _____________.
solution: In Luke 10:1, Jesus chose and sent 'seventy disciples' (or 72 in some manuscripts) ahead of him on a mission to 'every town and place where he himself was about to visit.'

options.a: every town where he was to visit
options.d: plant seeds in the vine yard

question: Paul was accused of apparently being a preacher of foreign divinities in Athens because he preached Jesus and the
solution: Acts 17:182 records that some Epicurean and Stoic philosophers in Athens called Paul a 'proclaimer of foreign divinities' because he was preaching specifically about 'Jesus and the resurrection.'

question: The laying of hands on Barnabas and Paul for the First Missionary Journey signifies their being
solution: In Acts 13:2-3, the 'laying of hands' on Barnabas and Paul by the church at Antioch was a formal act showing they were 'commissioned and set apart' for the first missionary journey.

question: “The sword devours now one and now another.” This statement was David’s reaction to the death of __________.
solution: In 2 Samuel 11:25, David's cold reaction to the news of Uriah the Hittite's death was 'the sword devours now one and now another,' attempting to mask his own role in Uriah's murder.

question: Paul’s teaching in Colossians on love, respect and obedience concerns
solution: In Colossians 3:18-21, Paul provides specific teachings on domestic and 'family relationships', addressing the duties of wives, husbands, children, and parents to one another.

question: How old was Joshua before he died?
solution: The book of Joshua (Joshua 24:29-30) records that Joshua 'the servant of the Lord' died at the age of 'one hundred and ten years' (110) after faithfully leading Israel.

question: For how long did the Lord supply the children of Israel with manna in the wilderness?
solution: In Exodus 16:35, the Bible states that the children of Israel 'ate manna for forty years' until they reached the border of the land of Canaan, where the supply finally ceased.

question: The major reason God allowed the Babylonian attack on the city of Jerusalem was to
solution: Prophets like Jeremiah explained that 'God allowed the Babylonian attack' (including the exile) primarily to 'punish the people of Judah and their leaders for their persistent sins' and idolatry.

options.a: demonstrate the ability of God to deliver His people from captivity
options.b: set a trap for the Babylonians and destroy them
options.c: restore the people of Judah and their leaders to the true worship of God
options.d: punish the people of Judah and their leaders for their sins.

question: The sun stood still when
solution: Joshua 10:12-14 records that the 'sun stood still' and the moon stopped for a whole day while 'Joshua defeated the five kings of the Amorites at Gibeon.'

options.a: Moses led the Israelites out of Egypt
options.b: Jericho fell to the army of Joshua
options.c: Noah came out of the ark
options.d: Joshua defeated the Amorites at Gibeon

question: “Am I God, to kill and to make alive, that this man sends words to me to cure a man of his leprosy...” Prophet Elisha responded to the king’s reaction in the statement above by
solution: When the King of Israel reacted with despair to the King of Syria's request for help (2 Kings 5), Elisha sent word to the king saying 'Let him (the leper Naaman) come to my house' so he may know a prophet is in Israel.

options.a: directing the nation of Israel to pray and fast
options.b: requesting the leper to come to his house
options.c: directing the leper to go to River Jordan
options.d: asking the leper to offer a sacrifice.

question: “I will not eat until I have told my errand” In the statement above, the errand of Abraham’s servant was to
solution: In Genesis 24, Abraham's senior servant (traditionally Eleazar) told the family of Rebekah 'I will not eat until I have told my errand,' which was his 'mission to find a wife for Isaac.'

question: Who was the first king of Israel?
solution: Saul (the son of Kish) was the first king to rule over a united Israel, anointed by the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 10:1).

question: According to Ephesians, children who obey their parents in the Lord will
solution: In Ephesians 6:1-3, Paul reminds children that 'obeying parents in the Lord' is the first commandment with a promise: 'that it may go well with you and you may have long life on the earth.'

question: According to Romans, all who were baptized into Christ were also baptized into His
solution: In Romans 6:3, Paul explains that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into His 'death', symbolizing the burying of the old self to walk in newness of life.

question: When Eve was about to be created, God did all the following except.
solution: In the second creation story (Genesis 2:21-22), God caused a deep sleep to fall on Adam, took a rib from his side, and 'closed up its place with flesh' (not a bone) to create Eve.
