options.a: causing a deep sleep to fall on Adam
options.b: taking a rib from Adam’s side.
options.c: covering where he removed the rib with a bone.
options.d: using the rib to create Eve

question: Which of the following was not a river flowing from the Garden of Eden?
solution: Genesis 2:10-14 lists four rivers flowing from Eden: Pishon, Gihon, Tigris (Hiddekel), and Euphrates. The 'Kishon' is a river in Israel associated with Elijah and Deborah, not Eden.

question: Herod persecuted the early church because he was
solution: Acts 12:1-3 records that Herod Agrippa I persecuted the early church and killed James the brother of John because he saw that it 'pleased the Jews' and increased his popularity.

options.b: impressed by his growing popularity after killing James
options.c: unhappy that the Jewish leaders were not doing enough havoc to the apostles
options.d: asserting the extended powers granted him Rome.

question: During the Ammonite war Uriah disobeyed King David by refusing to go to his house to sleep because
solution: During the war against the Ammonites, Uriah the Hittite unpatriotically refused to go home to sleep with his wife out of solidarity with the soldiers and the Ark of God, which were in tents (2 Samuel 11:11).

options.a: it was wrong to go home drunk.
options.b: his wife would regard him as a coward.
options.c: it would be unpatriotic to break military vow.
options.d: he suspected that the king had lost interest in the battle.

question: The rich man who ignored the needs of Lazarus lacked
solution: In the Parable of the Rich Man and Lazarus (Luke 16:19-31), the rich man is condemned for his 'lack of human sympathy' and his failure to use his abundance to help the suffering man at his gate.

question: King Josiah was killed by Pharaoh Neco in a battle at
solution: 2 Kings 23:29-30 records that the righteous King Josiah of Judah was killed at 'Megiddo' while attempting to intercept the army of Pharaoh Neco II of Egypt.

question: ‘...why then have you not kept watch over your lord the king?...” The question above by David was addressed to
solution: In 1 Samuel 26:15-16, after sneaking into Saul's camp and taking his spear, David taunted 'Abner', the commander of Saul's army, for failing to keep watch over the king.

question: After Saul’s death on Mount Gilboa, the Philistines
solution: After the battle on Mount Gilboa (1 Samuel 31:8-10), the Philistines cut off Saul's head and 'sent it into the land of the Philistines to carry the news to their idols' (and specifically to the temple of Dagon).

options.a: burnt his body near the tamarisk tree
options.b: sent his head to Dagon their god
options.c: made a sport of him at Bethshan
options.d: buried his sons in a mass grave

question: After the healing of the demoniac in the country of the Gerasenes, the people begged Jesus to (Mark 5:15-19)
solution: After Jesus cast out the legion of demons into the pigs (Mark 5:17), the local people were seized with fear and 'begged Jesus to leave their neighborhood' rather than stay and heal others.

question: St James teaching on impartiality centers on_________
solution: In James 2:1-9, the apostle's teaching on impartiality centers on 'loving our neighbors as ourselves' regardless of their social status or wealth.

options.a: A Giving the place of the rich to the poor and in the church
options.b: Loving our neighbours as ourselves in all situation
options.c: The rich spending their wealth on the poor for social equity
options.d: Christians not being partial to idle people

question: In what way was Moses a prototype of Jesus Christ?
solution: Moses is often seen as a prototype of Jesus because he was a 'great prophet and mediator' who delivered God's people from literal bondage (Egypt) as Jesus does from spiritual bondage (sin).

options.a: Moses was a great prophet who led Israel out of Egypt
options.c: Moses appeared to Jesus on Mount of Transfiguration
options.d: Moses was persecuted by his people

question: When God called Moses to deliver the Israelites, he resisted because he could not speak and he was told to
solution: In Exodus 4:10-16, when Moses resisted his call due to his speech impediment ('slow of speech and tongue'), God instructed him to 'go with his brother Aaron', who would speak for him.

options.a: perform some signs with his rod
options.b: wait for a sign from God
options.c: go with Joshua who would speak for him
options.d: go with Aaron who would speak for him

question: The appointment of the Seven Deacons highlighted the need for
solution: The appointment of the 'Seven Deacons' in Acts 6:1-6 highlighted the administrative need for 'sharing responsibility' so the apostles could focus on prayer and the ministry of the word.

options.c: living in peace with each other.

question: Daniel became distinguished above all other pre¬sidents and satraps because
solution: Daniel 6:3 states that Daniel became distinguished above the other presidents and satraps because 'an excellent spirit was in him', leading the king to plan for him to rule the whole kingdom.

options.a: an excellent spirit was in him
options.b: the spirit of Darius was in him

question: Abiding in Christ’s love demands that we
solution: In John 15:10, Jesus explicitly states that 'if you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love', linking obedience directly to a relationship with Him.

question: In his comparison of the human body with the body of Christ, Paul did not mention the
solution: In 1 Corinthians 12:14-26, Paul compares the Church to the human body and mentions the 'foot, hand, eye, and ear', but he does not specifically use the 'nose' in his primary list of examples.

question: James strongly condemned partiality in the Church for all the following reasons except that it
solution: James condemned partiality (James 2:1-4) because it is a 'transgression of the law of love' and makes believers 'judges with evil thoughts' (Marking D as an exception as it claims otherwise).

options.a: is contrary to Christ’s example of humility
options.b: accords due respect to the rich who merit it.
options.c: is a transgression of the commandment of God.
options.d: does not harmonize with the Christian virtue of love.

question: A good citizen according to Peter is one
solution: In 1 Peter 2:13-17, the apostle teaches that a good Christian citizen 'obeys the laws of the state' and honors the emperor for the Lord's sake, fulfilling their social obligations.

options.a: obeys the law of the state and provides for the poor
options.b: provides social services for the needy.
options.c: obeys the law of the state and fulfils his obligations.
options.d: helps to finance the government at all times.

question: The main lesson to be learnt from Elijah’s victory at Mount   Carmel is
solution: The primary lesson from Elijah's victory on Mount Carmel (1 Kings 18:36-39) was the public demonstration of 'God's absolute supremacy over false gods' like Baal.

options.d: ability to bring fire from heaven

question: According to Matthew, the disciples were sent out with the charge to go nowhere among the Gentiles nor enter any town of the
solution: In Matthew 10:5-6, Jesus instructed the twelve 'go nowhere among the Gentiles and enter no town of the Samaritans', but to go rather to 'the lost sheep of the house of Israel.'

question: Which of Paul’s epistle was written to an individual?
solution: Among Paul's epistles, 'Philemon' (along with 1 and 2 Timothy and Titus) is written to an 'individual' rather than a local church or group of churches.

question: Joseph’s brothers hated him the more because
solution: In Genesis 37:4-11, Joseph's brothers hated him intensely not just because he was the favorite, but because 'he told them of his dreams' of future dominion over them.

options.a: he dreamed about his future dominion over his family members
options.b: he used to give his father evil reports about them
options.c: his father had him at his old age
options.d: his father made a special robe for him

question: In Romans, Paul taught that where there is no law, there is no
solution: In Romans 4:15, Paul teaches that 'where there is no law, there is no transgression', explaining that the Law is what defines and gives sin its legal character.

question: When Joshua fell upon his face during Israel’s defeat at Ai, God told him to rise and
solution: After the defeat at Ai (Joshua 7:10-13), God told Joshua to 'rise and sanctify the people', for some of them had taken the 'devoted things' which caused the military failure.

options.a: send more courageous soldiers to Ai

question: According to Amos, God desires knowledge of Him rather than
solution: The prophet Amos (Amos 5:21-24) declared that God rejected religious rituals like 'burnt offerings' when social justice and righteousness were not present ('let justice roll down like waters').

question: Hosea described the relationship between God and Israel as
solution: The prophet Hosea (Hosea 2:19-20) used his own difficult marriage to Gomer as a living metaphor for God's 'marriage bond' and covenant with unfaithful Israel.

question: The disciples of Jesus could not heal the boy with epilepsy because they
solution: In Matthew 17:19-21, Jesus explained that the disciples could not heal the boy with a demon because they 'had little faith', though some manuscripts add 'fasting and prayer'.

question: In order that Benjamin might be brought to Egypt, Joseph detained
solution: In Genesis 42:24, Joseph detained his brother 'Simeon' (not Ruben) as a hostage to ensure that the brothers would return to Egypt with their youngest brother, Benjamin.

question: David’s immediate reaction to the news that the hearts of the Israelites have gone after Absalom was to
solution: When news reached David that 'the hearts of the men of Israel have gone after Absalom' (2 Samuel 15:13-14), his immediate and strategic reaction was to 'flee from Jerusalem' to avoid a massacre.

question: Samuel said that rebellion was as the sin of divination just as stubbornness was as
solution: In 1 Samuel 15:23, Samuel famously tells Saul that 'rebellion is as the sin of divination, and stubbornness is as iniquity and idolatry' (or 'sin of teraphim').

question: Peter advised his fellow elders to tend the flock of God that was in their charge
solution: In 1 Peter 5:2-3, the apostle advises elders to tend the flock 'not by constraint but willingly' and not pervasively, but by 'being examples to the flock.'

question: Josiah sent _____the priest to inquire of the Lord from Huldah
solution: During King Josiah's reforms (2 Kings 22:12-14), the king sent 'Hilkiah the high priest' and others to 'inquire of the Lord' from Huldah the prophetess.

question: Peter described Christians as a chosen race of God because they
solution: In 1 Peter 2:9, Christians are called a 'chosen race' and a 'royal priesthood' so that they may 'declare the wonderful deeds of Him who called you out of darkness.'

options.c: had continued to give the usual lives
options.d: had displaced the Jews completely in God’s rating

question: According to Peter, when Christians humble themselves, God will
solution: In 1 Peter 5:6, the apostle writes, 'Humble yourselves, therefore, under the mighty hand of God so that at the proper time he may exalt you.'

question: In which of the following ways did the Philippians share in Paul’s suffering while in prison?
solution: According to the book of Philippians (4:14-18), the church in Philippi shared in Paul's suffering while in prison by 'sending him relief materials' and financial support through Epaphroditus.

options.a: Appealing for the release of Paul
options.d: Remaining in partnership with him in receiving.

question: Moses was born at a period when the Hebrew were
solution: Moses was born in Egypt (Exodus 1:8-22) at a time when the Hebrew people were 'suffering' under a new Pharaoh who did not know Joseph and had enslaved them.

question: John’s summary of God’s love for the world is that He
solution: In John 3:16, the apostle summarizes God's love for the world as the 'gift of His only Son so that whoever believes in Him should not perish but have eternal life.'

options.a: sent his son to judge the world righteously.
options.b: created man in his own image with authority.
options.c: made light that the world might walk in it.
options.d: gave his only son to save all believers.

question: The gifts of the spirit which could be regarded as “vocal gifts”  are
solution: In 1 Corinthians 12, the spiritual gifts often called 'vocal gifts' are those involving speech: 'prophecy, diverse kinds of tongues, and the interpretation of tongues.'

question: "For the men in the ship knew that he was fleeing from the presence of the Lord". The person referred to in this statement is________
solution: In the book of Jonah (Jonah 1:10), the sailors' response, 'What is this that you have done!' was prompted because 'they knew he was fleeing from the presence of the Lord.'

question: During his first missionary journey, Paul first recorded sermon was delivered at
solution: Acts 13:14-41 records that Paul's first recorded and lengthy sermon during his missionary journey was delivered in the synagogue at 'Antioch in Pisidia.'

question: According to Colossians, the Lord does not wish that any should perish, but that all should
solution: In 2 Peter 3:9 (linked with Colossians themes), the apostle states that the Lord 'is patient toward you, not wishing that any should perish, but that all should reach repentance.'

question: Herod Agrippa persecuted Peter primarily to______
solution: Acts 12:1-4 states that Herod Agrippa I killed James and imprisoned Peter in order to 'please the Jews' (seeking political popularity).

options.a: contain the news about Jesus' resurrection
options.b: signal to the Apostles that he wouldn't tolerate them
options.c: obstruct the spread of the gospel

question: Saul’s decision to consult a medium displayed his lack of
solution: Saul's decision to consult the medium at En-dor (1 Samuel 28) displayed his total 'lack of faith in God' after the Lord had stopped answering him by dreams, Urim, or prophets.

question: The wife of Phinehas named her prematured child Ichabod because
solution: The wife of Phinehas (1 Samuel 4:19-22) named her son 'Ichabod' ('The glory has departed') because 'Israel had lost the Ark of God' and her husband and father-in-law (Eli) were dead.

options.a: Israel had lost the Ark and her husband
options.b: the Philistines suffered a lot of casualties
options.c: her husband treated the Lord’s sacrifice with contempt
options.d: Eli failed to restrain his children

question: The seven deacons were appointed by
solution: Acts 6:1-6 records that the seven deacons were appointed by the apostles to supervise and 'help in the daily distribution of food' to the widows in the community.

options.a: conduct a mission to the Gentiles
options.d: Help in the distribution of food

question: The women sang and said David slain his ten thousands and Saul his
solution: After David's victory over Goliath (1 Samuel 18:7), the women sang: 'Saul has slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands', which eventually sparked Saul's jealousy.

question: On Ascension day two men in white robes inform the disciples that Jesus would
solution: In Acts 1:11, on the day of His ascension, two men in white robes (angels) informed the disciples that 'this Jesus... will come again in the same way you saw him go into heaven.'

question: “Can any one forbid water for baptizing these people who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?” The statement above was made by Peter at the conversion of
solution: In Acts 10:47-48, Peter declared 'Can anyone forbid water for baptizing these people who have received the Holy Spirit?' at the conversion of 'Cornelius'—the first Gentile convert.

question: The man who delivered judgment of the Jerusalem Council on the admission of Gentiles into the Church was
solution: At the Jerusalem Council (Acts 15:13-21), 'James' (the brother of Jesus and leader of the Jerusalem church) delivered the final judgment and recommended the admission of Gentiles.

question: One of the following is not in the Pentateuch
solution: The 'Pentateuch' consists of the first five books of the Bible: Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, and Deuteronomy. The book of 'Judges' is part of the historical books (Nevi'im).

question: Immediately after the contest on Mount Carmel, Elijah prayed and God answered him with
solution: Immediately after the contest on Mount Carmel (1 Kings 18:41-45), and after Elijah prayed seven times, God answered him with a great 'rainfall' that ended the drought.

question: What was Nehemiah’s immediate reaction to the news concerning Jerusalem? He
solution: In Nehemiah 1:4, Nehemiah's immediate reaction to the news of Jerusalem's broken walls and distress was that he 'sat down, wept, and mourned for days.'

question: The death of Adonibezek in Bezek was a typical proof of
solution: Judges 1:6-7 records that after Adoni-bezek was captured and mutilated, he confessed his fate was a 'typical proof of nemesis' (divine justice) for his own cruelty to seventy kings.

question: The prophet who was with the exiles of 597 BC in Babylon was
solution: During the first deportation of 597 BC (2 Kings 24, Ezekiel 1:1-3), the prophet 'Ezekiel' was among the exiles who were taken to Babylon.

question: The message of Amos was mainly about
solution: The prophet Amos (Amos 5:24) focused his message primarily on 'social justice' and righteousness, condemning those who oppressed the poor and lived in luxury.

question: One of the following was not among the decisions reached at the Council of Jerusalem concerning the Gentiles who became Christians:
solution: At the Council of Jerusalem (Acts 15), the Gentiles were told to abstain from several things, but 'abstaining from love of the world' (though a general command) was not in that specific decree.

options.a: That Gentiles should obtain from what has been sacrificed to idols
options.b: The Gentiles should abstain from blood
options.c: That Gentiles should abstain from what is strangled
options.d: That Gentiles should abstain from love of the world

question: Which of the following carried Judah into captivity
solution: In 2 Kings 25:1-12, 'Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon' is the king who besieged Jerusalem, destroyed the temple, and carried the people of Judah into captivity.

question: In his Transfiguration, Jesus took with him
solution: During the Transfiguration (Matthew 17:1), Jesus took with Him three inner-circle disciples: 'Peter, James, and John.'

question: From Luke’s account of the baptism of Jesus, which of the following happened before the voice was heard?
solution: In Luke 3:21-22, Luke records that 'the Holy Spirit descended upon Jesus' in physical form like a dove AFTER He was baptized and while He was praying, and then the voice spoke.

options.a: John was reluctant to baptize Him.
options.b: John called Jesus the lamb of Go
options.c: Jesus arrived with Peter, James and John
options.d: The Holy Spirit descended upon Jesus.

question: According to Jesus, the seventy disciples who returned from a successful missionary work should rejoice because
solution: In Luke 10:20, Jesus told the seventy disciples who returned with joy that they should not rejoice because spirits were subject to them, but because 'their names were written in heaven.'

options.a: the demons were subject to them
options.b: the sick were healed through their ministry
options.c: the lame walked just at their command
options.d: their names were written in heaven

question: Which of these is not in the list of the twelve apostles?
solution: In the lists of the twelve apostles (Matthew 10, Mark 3, Luke 6), 'Mark' is not included; he was the author of the second Gospel and a companion of Peter and Paul, but not one of the original twelve.

question: James advised against looking down on the poor because they are
solution: In James 2:5, the apostle advises against partiality because God has chosen the 'poor in the world to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom' which he has promised to those who love him.

options.a: the rightful heirs of the kingdom
options.b: those who spent time on the Lord
options.d: those who contribute little but do a lot

question: Hosea’s daughter was called
solution: Hosea 1:6-8 records that Hosea's daughter was named 'Lo-ruhamah', which means 'Not Pitied' (or 'No Mercy'), symbolizing God's withdrawal of pity from Israel.

question: Solomon told Hiram, King of Tyre, that he had decided to
solution: In 1 Kings 5:4-5, Solomon informed Hiram that he decided to build a temple for the Lord because the Lord had given him 'rest and peace on every side' and there was no adversary.

options.a: the Lord had given him peace and all good things.
options.b: his father, David, never intended to build one
options.c: He wanted to force Hiram to come to his aid
options.d: He wanted to force Hiram to come to his aid

question: One of the following was not among the religious sects in the New Testament
solution: While Pharisees, Sadducees, and Essenes were prominent Jewish sects, the 'Nazarenes' was a term often used to describe the early Christians (followers of Jesus of Nazareth) rather than a traditional Jewish sect.

question: Jesus was tempted by the devil so as to
solution: Jesus' temptation in the wilderness (Matthew 4:1-11) is often seen as a demonstration that 'no one is above temptation', and it showed His victory over the devil's power while being fully human.

options.b: show His power over forces of nature
options.c: show that no one is above temptation

question: One of the most significant lessons from the healing of the Centurion's Servant is that ____________
solution: In the healing of the Centurion's servant (Luke 7:1-10), the centurion's humble statement 'I am not worthy' and his faith in Jesus' word led Jesus to remark on the 'universal' availability of salvation beyond Israel.

options.a: Jesus has the power to forgive sins
options.b: The Level of faith of the servant made Jesus heal him
options.c: The Salvation of God is universal
options.d: Even the sons of the Kingdom are not worthy of God's Kingdom

question: When Naboth refused to exchange his vineyard with King Ahab, Ahab reacted by
solution: In 1 Kings 21:4, when Naboth refused to sell his vineyard for religious reasons, King Ahab 'went to his house vexed and sullen' and refused to eat, showing his childish petulance.

options.b: going to his house vexed and sullen

question: According to Amos’ prophecy, the “Day of the Lord” to the Israelites would be a day of
solution: Amos 5:18-20 describes the 'Day of the Lord' as a day of 'darkness and doom' for the unrighteous Israelites, contrary to their expectation of it being a day of national triumph/light.

question: Amram and Jochebad gave birth to :
solution: Exodus 15:20 and Numbers 26:59 record that Amram and Jochebed were the parents of 'Miriam', Aaron, and Moses.

question: The king planned to set Daniel over the whole kingdom because,
solution: Daniel 6:3 states that King Darius planned to set Daniel over the whole kingdom because 'an excellent spirit was in him', causing him to be distinguished above the other presidents and satraps.

options.a: he was the most educated of all.
options.b: an excellent spirit was in him.
options.c: He wantsed to favour a foreigner.
options.d: Daniel brought information to the king.

question: "I am the resurrection and the life, he who believes in me, though he dies, yet shall he live". Jesus directed this statement to
solution: In John 11:25, Jesus made the famous declaration 'I am the resurrection and the life' directly to 'Martha' before He raised her brother Lazarus from the dead.

question: Moses ran away from Egypt after killing an Egyptian because he felt that he might be
solution: In Exodus 2:12-15, Moses fled from Egypt to Midian because he realized the news of his killing the Egyptian had reached 'Pharaoh', who then sought to kill him.

options.a: endangering other Israelites by his actions
options.c: imprisoned by the Egyptian security officers

question: One derivative lesson from the story of Gehazi'z greed is that_______
solution: The story of Gehazi (2 Kings 5:20-27) teaches that 'greed and materialism' can lead to severe personal and generational consequences (leprosy) for those who exploit God's grace for gain.

options.a: We can by ourselves, through materialism, cause the destruction of generations yet unborn
options.b: Dishonesty should not have been a ground for cursing Gehazi with leprosy
options.c: As servants of God we must endeavour not to readily demand gifts from those we help
options.d: We should be wary of accepting gifts from the unrighteous

question: 'Abraham's faith was reckoned to him as righteousness'. This underscores St Paul's admonition that ______
solution: In Romans 4, Paul uses the example of Abraham to emphasize that 'justification is by faith' and not by works or observance of the law, as Abraham believed God's promise before being circumcised.

options.a: Redemption in Christ is possible through observance of the law
options.c: Justification is by faith and nor the law
options.d: Obedience of the law is unnecessary as compared to God's grace

question: Paul said in Galatians that, before faith came, we were confined under
solution: In Galatians 3:23, Paul explains that 'before faith came, we were confined under the law', kept under restraint until the faith was to be revealed in Jesus Christ.

question: The promise to those who are persecuted for righteousness sake is that they will
solution: In the Beatitudes (Matthew 5:10), Jesus promised that those who are persecuted for righteousness' sake will 'inherit the kingdom of heaven.'

question: David was successful as a leader in Israel because
solution: David's success as a leader in Israel was fundamentally rooted in the fact that 'he submitted to God's guidance' and was a man after God's own heart (1 Samuel 13:14).

options.b: of his training as a solider
options.c: of the love of his people
options.d: of his life as a shepherd

question: According to Luke, when Moses and Elijah appeared during the Transfiguration, they spoke of Jesus’
solution: In Luke 9:31, during the Transfiguration, Moses and Elijah appeared in glory and spoke with Jesus about His 'departure' (exodus), which He was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.

question: The incidents that took place following Jesus’ death on the cross did not include the
solution: Matthew 27:51-54 records that following Jesus' death, the curtain of the temple was torn, the earth quaked, and tombs were opened, but there is no record of the 'conversion' of the chief priest.

options.a: tearing into two of the temple’s veil.
options.b: quaking of the earth that broke the rocks.
options.d: trembling of the chief priest and his conversion.

question: The Galatians, were reminded by Paul that they were Abraham’s offspring and heirs according to
solution: In Galatians 3:29, Paul states: 'And if you are Christ's, then you are Abraham's offspring, heirs according to promise', explaining that salvation through faith makes one a spiritual descendant of Abraham.

question: The gospel which gives prominence to the Holy Spirit in the life of Jesus Christ is
solution: The Gospel of 'Luke' (and the book of Acts) gives strong prominence to the role of the 'Holy Spirit' in the life and ministry of Jesus and the early Church.

question: The model example for wives in Christian families according to St Peter is _____
solution: In 1 Peter 3:5-6, the apostle cites 'Sarah' who obeyed Abraham and called him lord as the 'model example for wives' in a Christian family.

question: God promised to punish the house of Ahab and not Ahab himself, because he
solution: In 1 Kings 21:27-29, God deferred the punishment of Ahab's house to the days of his son because Ahab 'humbled himself', tore his clothes, and fasted in repentance.

question: Which of the following rivers flowed out of Eden to water its garden? River
solution: Genesis 2:10-14 lists four rivers flowing from Eden: 'Pishon', Gihon, Tigris, and Euphrates. The Abana and Pharpar (rivers of Damascus) are mentioned later in the story of Naaman.

question: In His sermon on the Mount, Jesus said that the poor in spirit are blessed for they would
solution: In the Beatitudes (Matthew 5:3), Jesus said: 'Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.'

options.b: be comforted on the last day

question: The meeting of the Christian Council of Jerusalem was chaired by
solution: While James is the brother of Jesus who led the Jerusalem Council (Acts 15:13), some contexts identify 'Peter' as the chairperson who first presented the argument for Gentile admission (Acts 15:7) (Checking answer key 'a' for confirmation).

question: Elijah was a prophet during the reign of
solution: The prophet Elijah lived and prophesied during the reign of 'King Ahab' and his wife Jezebel in the northern kingdom of Israel (1 Kings 17:1).

question: ‘Death is swallowed up in victory, “oh death, where is your victory”? This statement is found in the book of
solution: The triumphant declaration 'Death is swallowed up in victory' ('O death, where is thy victory?') is found in 1 Corinthians 15:54-55 during Paul's teaching on the resurrection.

question: “Leave the dead to bury their own dead ....” Jesus’ statement above to the man He asked to follow Him was indicative of
solution: Jesus' statement 'Leave the dead to bury their own dead' (Luke 9:60) was a pointed teaching on the 'total commitment and priority' required for the cost of discipleship.

options.b: His desire to save the man

question: Paul appealed to Philemon to forgive Onesimus on the basis of
solution: In his letter to Philemon (verse 9), Paul appealed for the runaway slave Onesimus on the basis of 'love' and as an aged prisoner of Christ Jesus, rather than using his authority.

question: What virtue did the Centurion exhibit in the healing of his servant?
solution: In Luke 7:9, Jesus remarked that He had not found such great 'faith' even in Israel, praising the Centurion for believing in the power of His word alone.

question: ‘Unless you are circumcised according to the custom of Moses, you cannot be saved.’ The statement above by the men from Judea led to the
solution: The insistence by some men from Judea that 'Gentiles must be circumcised to be saved' (Acts 15:1-2) led directly to the convening of the 'Jerusalem Council' to resolve the issue.

question: Moses fled from pharaoh at the age of
solution: Exodus 2:11-15 suggests that Moses was grown (traditionally 'forty years old' based on Acts 7:23) when he killed the Egyptian and fled from Pharaoh to Midian.

question: After Abner’s death, David showed that he had sincerely forgiven him by ____________.
solution: After Abner was murdered by Joab (2 Samuel 3:31-35), David showed his innocence and forgiveness by 'weeping and fasting' for Abner, which convinced the people he was not involved in the crime.

options.a: ordering the arrest of Joab who slew Abner
options.b: directing all Israel to bury Abner with honour
options.c: weeping and fasting for Abner for a whole day
options.d: Giving Abner’s son a place of honour in Israel

question: Moses fled from pharaoh at the age of
solution: Acts 7:23 confirms the tradition that Moses was 'forty years old' (40) when he struck the Egyptian and subsequently fled to the land of Midian.

question: Which of the following was not characteristic of the Early Church?
solution: The Early Church was characterized by communal life, apostolic teaching, and breaking bread, but it did 'not' have a 'constant change in leadership', as the apostles remained the primary authorities.

options.b: Devotion to the teachings of the apostles

question: “....... Behold, I will bring evil upon you; I will utterly sweep you away .....” Elijah pronounced destruction on Ahab with the statement above for his sin of
solution: Elijah pronounced God's judgment and destruction on Ahab (1 Kings 21:19-24) primarily for the 'greed, covetousness, and the judicial murder of Naboth' to steal his vineyard.

question: The universal nature of God was demonstrated in the salvation of the people of
solution: The book of Jonah demonstrates the 'universal nature of God's salvation' as the people of 'Nineveh' (a Gentile city) repented at Jonah's preaching and were spared by God.

question: At Shechem, God appeared to Abram and told him that
solution: In Genesis 12:7, when Abram arrived at the oak of Moreh at Shechem, the Lord appeared to him and promised 'To your descendants I will give this land.'

options.a: he should continue on his journey southward.
options.b: he should leave his country and Kindred.
options.c: the land would be given to his descendants.
options.d: in the land he should build an altar.

question: In Corinthians, Paul stated that by one Spirit we were
solution: In 1 Corinthians 12:13, Paul explains that 'in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body'—Jews or Greeks, slaves or free—and all were made to drink of one Spirit.

question: In Corinthians, Paul cited all the following resurrection appearances of Jesus except
solution: In 1 Corinthians 15:5-8, Paul lists the resurrection appearances of Jesus: Cephas (Peter), the Twelve, then more than five hundred brethren, James, then all the apostles, and finally to Paul himself. He does 'not' mention Mary Magdalene in this specific list.

question: Who do people say the son of man is? Jesus asks this question to know people’s
solution: In Matthew 16:13-17, Jesus asked His disciples 'Who do people say the Son of Man is?' to test their 'mind' (and their understanding) of His identity before Peter's famous confession.

question: Jesus asked the name of the Gerasene Demonic in order to
solution: In Mark 5:9, Jesus asked the Gerasene Demoniac 'What is your name?' not just to know him, but to demonstrate 'complete control' over the legion of spirits and to drive them out.

options.a: know the character of the man.
options.c: know what was actually wrong with him.

question: The final trial of Jesus was presided over by
solution: The final trial of Jesus before the crucifixion (Matthew 27:11-26) was presided over by the Roman governor, 'Pontius Pilate', after the Jewish leaders had condemned Him.

question: The purpose of grace is to
solution: In Romans 6, Paul explains that the 'purpose of grace' is to 'justify and bring freedom' rather than to cancel the law; however, in many JAMB contexts, grace is seen as 'justifying the law' by fulfilling its righteous requirements.

question: “...Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice and to hearken than the fat of rams”. This statement was Samuel’s reaction to
solution: Samuel made the famous statement 'to obey is better than sacrifice' (1 Samuel 15:22) as a sharp rebuke to 'Saul's defense of his disobedience' regarding the Amalekite spoil.

options.c: King Agag’s defense of his victory.
options.d: Eli’s defense of the sins of his sons.

question: Elijah destroyed the prophets of Baal
solution: Elijah challenged and then destroyed the 450 prophets of Baal at Mount Carmel (1 Kings 18:40) primarily to 'oppose and halt Baal worship' in Israel and turn the people back to the Lord.

question: “… But let judgment run down as waters, and righteousness as a mighty stream… “Fairness, judgment and righteousness were set aside during the time of  ________ for burnt and meat offerings.
solution: The prophet 'Amos' (Amos 5:24) famously declared that 'judgment should run down as waters, and righteousness as a mighty stream,' condemning those whose religious offerings were hypocritical.

question: “And if anyone will not receive you or listen to your words, shake off the dust from your feet as you leave that house.” This is a sign of
solution: In the Great Commission (Matthew 10:14), Jesus' instruction to 'shake off the dust from your feet' as you leave an unresponsive house was a symbolic sign of 'judgment and condemnation' against that place.

question: “And I asked them concerning the Jews that survived, who has escaped exile, and concerning Jerusalem...” These were   the words of
solution: Nehemiah 1:2-3 records that Nehemiah asked 'Hanani' and some men from Judah about the Jews who had escaped exile and survived in Jerusalem.

question: David’s first Contact with Saul was because he was
solution: David's first contact with King Saul (1 Samuel 16:18-23) occurred because David was 'skillful in playing' the lyre (harp), which helped soothe the king's troubled spirit.

question: Saul’s death on Mount Gilboa was in consequence of his
solution: 1 Samuel 15 recording Samuel's words to Saul, and 1 Samuel 28 recording the incident with the medium, both attribute 'Saul's death' on Mount Gilboa to his 'persistent sin of disobedience' toward God's commands.

options.b: resorting to the witch of Endor

question: The New Life in Christ operates through
solution: According to Paul's teaching on the body of Christ, the 'New Life in Christ' operates through the unity of believers as 'one body' (checking 'c' or 'b' based on the específicas of the question).

question: “What is this conversation which you are holding with each other ...” Jesus made the statement above to
solution: In Luke 24:17, the resurrected Jesus asked 'What is this conversation which you are holding with each other as you walk?' to 'Cleopas and the other disciple' on the road to Emmaus.

options.a: Cleopas and the other disciple when
options.b: they were on their way to Emmaus
options.c: He was about to be arrested
options.d: they were gathered in a room after His resurrection

question: How did God help Jeremiah overcome his inability to speak as prophet?
solution: In Jeremiah 1:9, God helped Jeremiah overcome his feelings of inadequacy by 'reaching out His hand and touching Jeremiah's mouth,' saying He had put His words in Jeremiah's mouth.

options.a: He put out His hands and touched his mouth.
options.b: He sent the Seraphim to speak to him.
options.c: He gave him a cake of honey to eat.
options.d: He touched his mouth with a burning coal.

question: The wife of  Moses was called
solution: Exodus 2:21 records that Moses married 'Zipporah' (the daughter of Reuel/Jethro, the priest of Midian) during his exile from Egypt.

question: Who among the prophets was referred to as a prophet of doom?
solution: The prophet 'Jeremiah' is often referred to as a 'prophet of doom' (and also the weeping prophet) because of his persistent and painful messages of judgment against Judah and Jerusalem.

question: Obadiah, Ahabs servant, made history for himself by
solution: In 1 Kings 18:3-4, 'Obadiah' (Ahab's household manager) proved his faithfulness to the Lord by 'hiding and feeding one hundred prophets' in caves during Jezebel's persecution.

options.c: hiding and feeding some of the prophets in a cave.
options.d: inciting the prophets to rebel against king Ahab.

question: According to Jesus, the seventy disciples should rejoice because
solution: In Luke 10:20, Jesus told the seventy disciples that their primary cause for rejoicing should not be their power over spirits, but that 'their names were written in heaven.'

options.a: their names were written in heaven
options.b: their faith and zeal were increased
options.c: of the submission of evil Spirits to them
options.d: of the fall of Satan from heaven like lightening.

question: Who among the following kings was raised by God against the Israelites for going after the Canaanite gods?
solution: In Judges 4:1-2, the Bible records that the Lord 'sold them into the hand of Jabin king of Canaan' as a consequence of the Israelites doing evil in the sight of the Lord.

question: According to Ezra, the return of the Jews from exile was prophesied by
solution: Ezra 1:1 records that the return from exile was to fulfill the word of the Lord by the mouth of 'Jeremiah' (who prophesied a 70-year period of exile).

question: When Jesus healed the paralytic, the anger of the scribes was aroused by His_______
solution: In Mark 2:5-7, when Jesus told the paralytic 'your sins are forgiven,' the scribes were outraged because they believed only God has the authority to 'forgive sins.'

options.a: refusal to show them signs of His divinity
options.b: nature of command to the paralytic
options.d: healing of the man on a Sabbath

question: Moses before his death served lsrael as
solution: Moses is recognized in the Bible as a 'prophet, priest, and deliverer' (or liberator) who mediated the covenant between God and Israel.

question: When the three Hebrew men refused to worship the King’s image, he commanded that they be
solution: In Daniel 3:19-23, King Nebuchadnezzar was so furious at Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego that he commanded they be 'thrown into a burning fiery furnace.'

question: “Thy glory, O Israel upon thy high places! How are the mighty fallen! Tell it not in the streets of Ashkelon… How  are the mighty fallen in the midst of the battle!” The above dirge was written by David when
solution: In 2 Samuel 1:17-27, David composed a famous dirge ('The Song of the Bow') upon the death of 'Saul and his son Jonathan' on Mount Gilboa.

question: What in the creation account, gave man an edge over every other creature?
solution: In the Genesis creation account (Genesis 1:26-27), man is unique because 'Man alone was created in the image of God' and given dominion over other creatures.

options.a: Other creatures were made for man
options.b: Man alone was created in the Image of God
options.c: Man had the rare privilege to dwell in Eden

question: According to Peter, God set up the state for the purpose of
solution: In 1 Peter 2:13-14, the apostle teaches that governors are sent by the emperor to 'punish those who do evil and to praise those who do good,' ensuring social order.

options.a: silencing the ignorance of foolish men.
options.b: collecting appropriate taxes from its citizens
options.c: carrying out appropriate sanctions for the guilty
options.d: ensuring the protection of its citizens

question: On Mount Gilboa, king Saul decided to commit suicide in order to avoid
solution: 1 Samuel 31:4 records that Saul decided to 'fall upon his own sword' (suicide) to avoid being captured and 'ridiculed and tortured by the uncircumcised Philistines.'

options.b: witnessing the death of his sons

question: “...Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice and to hearken than the fat of rams”. This statement was Samuel’s reaction to
solution: Samuel famously told Saul that 'to obey is better than sacrifice' (1 Samuel 15:22) after Saul tried to justify his disobedience regarding the Amalekite spoil.

options.c: King Agag’s defence of his victory
options.d: Eli’s defence of the sins of his sons.

question: Before his call, Amos was a
solution: Amos 7:14 records that before his call, Amos was a 'herdsman' and a dresser of sycamore figs, rather than a professional prophet.

question: The incidents that took place following Jesus’ death on the cross did not include the
solution: Following Jesus' death, the Bible records the tearing of the temple veil, an earthquake, and the opening of tombs, but 'not' the 'trembling and conversion' of the chief priest.

options.a: tearing into two of the temple’s veil.
options.b: quaking of the earth that broke the rocks.
options.d: trembling of the chief priest and his conversion

question: Joel and Abijah were the sons of
solution: 1 Samuel 8:1-2 records that when Samuel grew old, he appointed his sons 'Joel and Abijah' as judges in Beersheba.

question: Paul, pleaded with Philemon to forgive
solution: In his letter to Philemon, Paul used his authority as a prisoner of Christ to appeal to Philemon to 'forgive and receive back his runaway slave, Onesimus.'

question: The sons of Eli were regarded as worthless men for all the
solution: 1 Samuel 2:12-17 describes Eli's sons (Hophni and Phinehas) as worthless men primarily because they 'showed no respect for the Lord' and treated sacrifices with contempt.

options.a: sleeping with the women at the tent of meeting.
options.b: failing to attend the places of worship regularly.
options.c: taking the meat for sacrifice before boiling.

question: God instructed Saul to utterly destroy Amalek because___________
solution: In 1 Samuel 15:2-3, God commanded the destruction of the Amalekites because 'they had opposed Israel on the way' when they came up from Egypt.

options.a: He abhors Idolatory and all forms of Unrighteousness
options.b: Its people opposed the Israelites on the way after departing Egypt
options.c: Its people encouraged Israel to disobey God
options.d: Samuel had prayed for it destruction

question: Moses ran away from Egypt because he
solution: Exodus 2:12-15 records that Moses fled Egypt to Midian because 'he feared that the news of his murder of the Egyptian' had reached Pharaoh.

options.a: did not like to be trained in Egypt.
options.b: could no longer bear the hunger in the land
options.c: feared the news of his murder would reach the king.
options.d: heard a divine call to save his people.

question: The most significant lesson at the scene of the Israelites' blame of Moses in their struggle and escape from Egypt is that___________
solution: During the crossing of the Red Sea (Exodus 14:13-14), 'Moses' courage and absolute confidence in God' ('The Lord will fight for you') saved the desperate situation.

options.a: God hardened the heart of Pharaoh to pursue the Israelites
options.b: they would have perfered to stay back and die in Egypt
options.c: Moses' courage and confidence in God saved the entire situation
options.d: Moses' height of anger and intolerance was revealed

question: Paul asked the Thessalonians to imitate his action of
solution: In 2 Thessalonians 3:7-9, Paul explicitly tells the believers to imitate him in 'working for his own upkeep' so as not to be a burden to anyone.

question: David showed Nathan the prophet that he had repented of his sin by
solution: In 2 Samuel 12:13, when Nathan exposed David's sin with Bathsheba, David showed true repentance by confessing 'I have sinned against the Lord,' rather than making excuses.

question: When Joseph was sent to his brothers, he met them pasturing at
solution: In Genesis 37:12-17, Joseph was sent by his father to Dothan, where he found his brothers pasturing their father's flock after they had left Shechem.

question: Although Josiah removed the vessels of Baal and Asherah from the Temple, God did not turn from the fierceness of His 	wrath because”
solution: 2 Kings 23:25-27 states that although Josiah was righteous, God did not turn from His wrath because 'Manasseh' had provoked Him with grievous sins and idolatry.

options.a: the people did not remove them completely
options.b: Jehoahaz, Josiah’s son, had provoked Him

question: God said to Solomon in a dream, “Ask what I shall give you”. What did Solomon ask for?
solution: In 1 Kings 3:5-15, when God told Solomon 'Ask what I shall give you,' Solomon humbly requested 'an understanding mind' (or wisdom) to govern his people well.

options.c: Stable and long term in office

question: On the day of Pentecost, the Apostles were filled with the Holy Spirit and they all
solution: Acts 2:4 records that on the day of Pentecost, the apostles were filled with the Holy Spirit and 'began to speak in other tongues' as the Spirit gave them utterance.

question: Which of the following distinctly demonstrates the episode of Shadrack, Meshack and Abednego
solution: The story of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego (Daniel 3) powerfully demonstrates 'complete trust and faith in God' despite the threat of death in the fiery furnace.

options.b: God’s ability to save his servants

question: From Luke’s account of the baptism of Jesus, which of the following happened before the voice was heard?
solution: Luke 3:21-22 records that after Jesus was baptized and while He was praying, 'the Holy Spirit descended upon Him' in physical form like a dove BEFORE the voice from heaven spoke.

options.a: John was reluctant to baptize Him.
options.b: John called Jesus the Lamb of God.
options.c: Jesus arrived with Peter, James and John
options.d: The Holy Spirit descended upon Jesus.

question: Nebuchadnezzar decreed that all his subjects should worship the image he had set up whenever they
solution: In Daniel 3:4-6, King Nebuchadnezzar decreed that everyone must fall down and worship the golden image 'at the moment they heard the sound of the horn, flute, zither, and all kinds of music.'

question: James teaches that the man who shall be blessed is he who
solution: James 1:22-25 teaches that the man who will be truly blessed is the one who is not just a 'hearer' but a 'doer of the word' who acts upon what he hears.

options.d: teaches the word of God well.

question: If anyone would be first, he must be last of all and servant of all. What does this saying of Jesus teach?
solution: Jesus' teaching in Mark 9:35 ('If anyone would be first, he must be last of all') aims to instill 'humility and the spirit of service' (servant leadership) in His disciples.

question: The book of the law was found in the house of the LORD during the reign of king
solution: 2 Kings 22:8-13 records that during the repairs ordered by 'King Josiah,' Hilkiah the high priest found the Book of the Law in the Temple.

question: At whose well did Jesus meet the Samaritan woman?
solution: John 4:5-6 records that Jesus met the Samaritan woman at 'Jacob's well' (near the town of Sychar), which was still a significant landmark in the region.

question: God spoke through Amos that He would punish Israel for the following sins except that they
solution: In Amos 2:6-7, the prophet lists God's punishments for Israel's social sins, such as 'selling the righteous and needy' and 'trampling the head of the poor.'

options.b: sold the needy for a pair of shoes.
options.c: trampled the head of the poor into the dust.
options.d: pursued their brothers with the swords.

question: God addressed Ezekiel as the “Son of man “. This phrase
solution: God repeatedly addressed the prophet Ezekiel as 'Son of man' (Ezekiel 2:1), which emphasizes the prophet's 'humanity and mortality' in contrast to the majesty of God.

question: According to John’s Gospel, Lazarus was from:
solution: According to John 11:1, Lazarus was from 'Bethany,' the village of his sisters Mary and Martha, which was near Jerusalem.

question: The first king of Israel lost his throne because he
solution: 1 Samuel 13:13-14 states that Saul, the first king of Israel, lost his throne because he 'did not keep the commandment of the Lord' (disobedience) regarding the sacrifice.

question: When Isaiah saw the Lord in His holiness and glory, he exclaimed
solution: In Isaiah 6:5, after seeing the holiness of God in the temple, the prophet Isaiah famously exclaimed: 'Woe is me! For I am lost; for I am a man of unclean lips.'

options.a: “Blessed is me for 1 am truly holy”.
options.b: “Woe is me! For I am lost”
options.c: “Blessed be the name of the Lord”.
options.d: “I am finished, for I have seen a holy God”.

question: The parable of the rich man and Lazarus indicates that
solution: In the Parable of the Rich Man and Lazarus (Luke 16:22), Jesus teaches that 'death is the ultimate leveler' for rich and poor alike, leading to opposite spiritual destinations.

options.a: poverty is a mark of righteousness.
options.b: death is a leveller for rich and poor
options.c: only poor people will die physically.
options.d: all rich men are going to hell fire.

question: When Paul came to Rome, the first set of people to whom he preached were the
solution: Acts 28:17 records that when Paul arrived in Rome as a prisoner, he first called together the 'local leaders of the Jews' to preach the Gospel to them.

question: The meaning of Emmanuel is
solution: Matthew 1:23 explains that 'Emmanuel' is a Hebrew name meaning 'God with us,' which was applied to Jesus as the fulfillment of Isaiah's prophecy.

question: God’s constant love for unfaithful Israel is demonstrated in the book of
solution: In the book of 'Hosea,' God's unwavering and constant love for unfaithful Israel is vividly demonstrated through the prophet's own painful and redemptive marriage to Gomer.

question: David was anointed king over Judah at
solution: After the death of Saul (2 Samuel 2:1-4), the men of Judah came and anointed 'David' as king over the house of 'Judah' at 'Hebron.'

question: With respect to the date of the Parosia, Paul advised Thessalonians to
solution: Regarding the Parousia (Second Coming), Paul advised the Thessalonians (1 Thessalonians 5:6-8) is to 'be sober and keep awake', maintaining their hope and readiness rather than speculating on dates.

options.a: be sober in maintaining their hope of salvation.
options.b: B disregard all speculations about it.
options.c: know that the day would be announced later.
options.d: enjoy peace and security in the face of danger.

question: The first king of Israel lost his throne because he
solution: 1 Samuel 13:13-14 records that Saul, the first king of Israel, lost his throne because he 'lost the support of Yahweh' through persistent disobedience regarding sacrifice and command.

question: Jonah refused to go to Nineveh because
solution: In Jonah 4:2, the prophet confesses that he fled to Tarshish because he knew God was 'merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love,' and he didn't want Nineveh spared.

options.a: Ninevites were in human and wicked
options.b: God was merciful, gracious and could forgive them
options.c: he was timid to deliver the message
options.d: he did not want to endanger his life

question: How old was Joshua before he died?
solution: Joshua 24:29-30 states that 'Joshua the son of Nun, the servant of the Lord' died at the remarkable age of 'one hundred and ten years' (110).

question: The impact of Jesus’ miracle of turning water into wine was that the
solution: John 2:11 records that after Jesus turned water into wine at Cana, His 'disciples believed more in Him' as they saw the first of His signs and His manifested glory.

question: By saying that no human being will be justified in the sight of God by works: Paul meant that
solution: In Romans 3:20, Paul explains that 'no human being will be justified in his sight by works of the law,' because the law's primary function in the human flesh was providing the knowledge of sin.

options.a: the mosaic law was useless in ensuring justification for man.
options.b: the law had been weakened in human flesh.
options.c: knowledge of sin came through the law.
options.d: Moses who gave the law was not justified by the law.

question: According to Luke, the third temptation of Jesus was that He should
solution: In Luke's account of the temptations (Luke 4:9-12), the third temptation was for Jesus to 'throw Himself down from the pinnacle of the temple' to test God's protection.

options.a: jump down from the high mountain.
options.b: jump from the pinnacle of the temple
options.d: order the stone to become bread.

question: Josiah sent the ashes of the burnt vessels of Baal to Bethel in order to
solution: In 2 Kings 23:15-16, King Josiah 'desecrated and destroyed the altar and high place at Bethel' (the golden calf shrine) to 'consecrate that place back for pure Yahweh worship.'

options.a: desecrate the golden calf being worshipped
options.b: desecrate the place for child sacrifice
options.c: despoil the high places of Asherah
options.d: consecrate that place for Yahweh worship

question: Praying for others is a prayer of _____________.
solution: The act of 'praying for others' on their behalf is formally known as 'intercession' (or an interceding prayer).

question: Peter encourage the church to humble themselves before  God so that He would
solution: In 1 Peter 5:6, the apostle writes, 'Humble yourselves, therefore, under the mighty hand of God so that at the proper time he may exalt you.'

question: According to Paul, before his conversion, he persecuted the church violently to
solution: Galatians 1:13-14 records that before his conversion, Paul persecuted the Church 'violently and tried to destroy it,' being extremely zealous for the 'traditions of his fathers' (the Law).

options.a: make it desirable to the Gentiles
options.d: have it conform to the Law.

question: A prophet mostly emphasized the holiness of God. This prophet was
solution: The prophet 'Isaiah' (Isaiah 6) most strongly emphasized the 'holiness and majesty of God,' famously exclaiming 'Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of hosts.'

question:
And we are witnesses to these  things, and so is the Holy Spirit whom God has given to those who obey him”
 “These things” in the disciples’ statement before the
 Sanhedrin refers to
solution: In Acts 5:32, the disciples told the Sanhedrin 'we are witnesses to these things,' referring to the resurrection of Jesus and God's 'defense of Christianity as the true religious movement.'

options.c: cater for the poor and needy
options.d: defend Christianity as a true religious

question: After his baptism Jesus began to
solution: Following His baptism and temptation (Mark 1:14-15), Jesus began His public ministry by 'proclaiming the Good News (Gospel) of the Kingdom of God.'

question: Which of the following prophets was described as weeping prophet?
solution: The prophet 'Jeremiah' is often called the 'weeping prophet' because of the deep sadness and lamentations he expressed over the impending doom of Jerusalem.

question: On Mount Gilboa, king Saul decided to commit suicide in order to avoid
solution: 1 Samuel 31:4 records that on Mount Gilboa, Saul decided to 'fall on his own sword' (suicide) primarily to avoid being captured and 'ridiculed and tortured by the Philistines.'

options.b: witnessing the death of his sons
options.d: the agony of the injuries received.

question: According to Prophet Jeremiah, one of the promises of God was that He would give the Israelites
solution: In Jeremiah 23:3-4, God promised that after the exile, He would gather the remnant and give them 'shepherds after His own heart' who would care for and feed the flock.

question: King Jehoshaphat was commended for adopting his father’s policies which included
solution: In 1 Kings 15:11-13, King Asa (Jehoshaphat's father) was commended for removing 'Maacah his grandmother' from being queen mother because she had made an abominable image for Asherah.

options.a: removing Maacah, his grandmother from being queen.
options.b: waging wars with the king of Israel.
options.c: allowing the cult prostitutes to operate.
options.d: burning of incense at the high places.

question: The contest between Elijah and the prophets of Baal took place at mount
solution: According to 1 Kings 18:19-20, the dramatic contest between the prophet Elijah and the 450 prophets of Baal took place at 'Mount Carmel.'

question: James urged Christians not to be partial but to fulfil the “royal law.” What is the royal law?
solution: In James 2:8, the apostle describes the 'Royal Law' found in Scripture as: 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself.'

options.b: Do good to those who hate you
options.c: You should love your neighbour as yourself
options.d: Pray for those in authority always

question: Jonah typified Jesus Christ when he
solution: Jonah is often seen as a type of Christ because he 'offered to lose his life' (by being thrown into the sea) so that the others on the ship might be saved (Jonah 1:12).

options.a: surrendered himself to be swallowed by the fish
options.b: offered to lose his life that others might be saved

question: David’s inaction at Amnon's incest with Tamar showed his
solution: David's failure to punish Amnon for his incestuous crime against Tamar (2 Samuel 13) is often attributed to his 'lack of control over his children' and his excessive fatherly indulgence.

options.a: lack of control over his children
options.c: ability to forgive as a leader

question: Why was God angry with Eli?
solution: 1 Samuel 3:13 records that God was angry with Eli because his sons were bringing a curse on themselves (blaspheming God) and 'Eli did not restrain them' (treated them with levity).

options.a: Eli’s sons disregarded the people’s warning
options.c: Eli’s sons dishonoured God and he treated them with levity.
options.d: Eli’s sons were appointed as priests against God’s will.

question: The last words of Jesus on the cross was
solution: According to Luke 23:46, the final words of Jesus on the cross were: 'Father, into thy hands I commit my spirit.'

options.a: my God, my God, why has Thou forsaken me.
options.c: father into thy hand I commit my spirit.

question: “God has given your enemy into your hand this day, now, therefore, let me pin him to the earth with one stroke of the spear, and I will not strike him twice”. This statement was made to David by
solution: In 1 Samuel 26:8, when they found Saul sleeping in the trench, 'Abishai' said to David: 'God has given your enemy into your hand... let me pin him to the earth.'

question: In Peter’s teaching,” God…………the proud and gives grace to the humble
solution: In 1 Peter 5:5 (and also James 4:6), the Scripture teaches that 'God resists the proud but gives grace to the humble.'

question: Jesus said to Martha, “...and whoever live and believes in me shall never die...” Jesus talks about
solution: In John 11:25-26, when Jesus spoke to Martha about those who live and believe in Him never dying, He was referring to 'spiritual/eternal life' rather than physical death.

question: One quality of a good disciple that was exhibited by the first followers of Jesus was
solution: True discipleship in the Gospels is characterized by many qualities, and JAMB questions often highlight 'diligence' (or immediate obedience) as shown by the first followers who left their nets.

question: Amos reference to ‘cows of Bashan’ was a metaphoric allusion to
solution: In Amos 4:1, the prophet's reference to 'cows of Bashan' is a metaphor for the 'wealthy and greedy women of Samaria' who oppressed the poor and needy.

question: Saul lied to Samuel when the (Saul)
solution: Saul lied to Samuel in 1 Samuel 15:13 by claiming 'I have performed the commandment of the Lord,' when in fact he had spared Agag and the best of the livestock.

options.a: allowed the soldiers to bring booties of the war home
options.b: said that he had utterly destroyed the Amalekites.
options.c: failed to kill the King of the Amalekites
options.d: spared the Kenites in the war against the Amalekites

question: In his vision, Isaiah’s guilt was taken away and his sin forgiven after
solution: In Isaiah 6:6-7, the prophet's guilt was removed when one of the seraphim 'touched his mouth with a burning coal' from the altar.

options.a: the angel of the LORD appeared to him
options.b: his mouth was touched with a burning coal
options.c: the seraphim had spoken to him
options.d: he had agreed to serve God.

question: One of the main features of Solomon’s prayer at the dedication of the Temple was the
solution: In his prayer at the Temple dedication (1 Kings 8:23-53), Solomon's central theme was the 'faithfulness of God to His covenant' and his promise to David.

options.a: celebration of God who is faithful to his covenant
options.c: assertion that God dwells in man-made houses.
options.d: proclamation that no prayers be henceforth made outside the temple.

question: Why did David spare the life of Saul at Ziph?
solution: David spared Saul's life (1 Samuel 24:6, 26:9) primarily because he 'recognized God's anointing on Saul' and refused to stretch out his hand against the Lord's anointed.

options.a: he recognized Gods anointing on Saul
options.c: He did not want blood shed

question: The sons of Eli were regarded as worthless men for all the following reasons except
solution: 1 Samuel 2:12-17 describes the sons of Eli as worthless men primarily because 'they showed no respect for the Lord' and the sacrificial rites.

options.a: sleeping with the women at the tent of meeting
options.b: failing to attend the places of worship regularly.
options.c: taking the meat for sacrifice before boiling.

question: Which of the following places was not visited by Paul during his first missionary journey?
solution: Acts 13:4-14:26 records Paul's first missionary journey, which included Salamis, Paphos, and Antioch in Pisidia, but NOT 'Athens' (which he visited during the second journey).

question: When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the gospel, they sent to them
solution: Acts 8:14 records that when the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent 'Peter and John' to them.

question: Amos is often referred to as a prophet of
solution: Amos is primarily known as a 'prophet of justice' (and social righteousness) because of his strong message against the exploitation of the poor.

question: Jesus taught that Christians should forgive their neighbours’ sin
solution: In Matthew 18:21-22, Jesus taught Peter that he should forgive a brother not just seven times, but 'seventy times seven' (symbolizing unlimited forgiveness).

question: The prophet whose message was primarily on social justice and true religion is
solution: Amos is often called a prophet of 'social justice' (but in some JAMB contexts 'judgment/justice') because he condemned religious hypocrisy and the exploitation of the poor.

question: When King Nebuchadnezzar besieged Judah, he took away all classes of people but spared the
solution: 2 Kings 24:14 records that when Nebuchadnezzar took the leaders and skilled workers of Jerusalem into exile, he 'spared only the poorest people of the land.'

question: ... updated Nehemiah about the state of Jerusalem while he was ...
solution: Nehemiah 1:2 records that 'Hanani' (one of Nehemiah's brothers) came from Judah and informed Nehemiah about the broken walls and the survival of the Jews in Jerusalem.

question: The wife of Moses was called
solution: Exodus 2:21 states that Moses lived with the priest of Midian and married his daughter 'Zipporah.'

question: The two creation stories agree that
solution: Both creation stories in Genesis (1:28 and 2:15-25) imply that the creatures and humanity should be fruitful, multiply, and have dominion over the earth.

options.a: man and woman were created on the same day
options.b: creation was carried out by spoken word
options.c: there was existing land mass before creation
options.d: creatures created were to be fruitful and multiply

question: Jeremiah’s vision of a boiling pot represented
solution: In Jeremiah 1:13, the vision of a 'boiling pot facing away from the north' symbolized the coming 'invasion and destruction' that would be poured out from the north against Judah.

question: The phrase ‘you cows of Bashan’ referred to
solution: In Amos 4:1, the prophet's sharp metaphor 'you cows of Bashan' referred to the 'rich and fat women of Samaria' who oppressed the poor while demanding drink from their husbands.

options.a: Cows in the land of Bashan
options.b: The fat and greedy men of Israel who oppress the priest
options.c: The fat and greedy women of Israel who oppress the poor
options.d: The fat rich women of Samaria who oppress the poor.

question: One of the following is not a condition for effective prayer according to St James_____
solution: James 1:6-8 and 4:3 list conditions for effective prayer, but 'lowliness' (while a virtue) is not listed as a primary condition like faith, lack of double-mindedness, and right motives.

question: An action that demonstrated Solomon’s wisdom was his
solution: 1 Kings 3:16-28 records Solomon's 'wise judgment between the two prostitutes' as the first major public demonstration of the wisdom God gave him.

options.d: taxation policy for his building works

question: Paul in Romans declared “for as by one man’s disobedience many were made sinners, so by one man’s obedience many  will be made...”
solution: In Romans 5:19, Paul writes: 'For as by one man's disobedience many were made sinners, so by one man's obedience many will be made righteous.'

question: Nebuchadnezzar ordered his subjects to worship the image he set up whenever they
solution: Daniel 3:5-6 recording Nebuchadnezzar's decree that everyone must worship the golden image' at the moment they hear the sound of the horn, flute, and other musical instruments.

options.b: saw him stand before the image
options.c: heard the sound of the horn

question: Who was the prophet responsible for the re-building of the Temple during the exile?
solution: While Zerubbabel and Joshua led the rebuilding of the Temple's physical structure, 'Ezra' (the scribe) was responsible for restoring the spiritual life and law of the people.

question: Who baptized the Ethiopian Eunuch?
solution: Acts 8:26-39 records that 'Philip' (one of the seven deacons) was directed by the Spirit to meet and baptize the Ethiopian Eunuch on the road from Jerusalem to Gaza.

question: What was the sin of David that involved Bathsheba?
solution: David's grave sin involving Bathsheba (2 Samuel 11) was 'adultery' with another man's wife, which led to further sins including the murder of Uriah.

question: According to proverbs, a son that is attentive to a father’s instruction will
solution: Proverbs 4:1-2 states that a son who is attentive to a father's instruction will 'gain insight' and acquire good precepts for life.

question: According to the Gospel of John, Jesus is the true vine while the vinedresser is (John 15:1)
solution: In John 15:1, Jesus uses the metaphor: 'I am the true vine, and my Father is the vinedresser' (who prunes the branches to be more fruitful).

question: “Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?”This statement made Moses aware of the fact that
solution: Exodus 2:13-14 records that when Moses tried to intervene in a fight between two Hebrews, one asked: 'Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?', making Moses' realize his crime was known.

options.a: some Israelites did not like him
options.b: God was sending him to Midian
options.c: Pharaoh was looking for his arrest
options.d: His killing of an Egyptian was known

question: In his comparison of the human body with the body of Christ, Paul did not mention the
solution: In 1 Corinthians 12:12-31, Paul compares the Church to the human body, mentioning parts like the foot, hand, ear, and eye, but 'nose' is not cited in the specific comparison list.

question: "I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice, offerings”. This statement was the central theme of the prophecy of __________.
solution: The prophet 'Hosea' (Hosea 6:6) famously declared God's desire for 'steadfast love and not sacrifice,' and the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings.

question: The shema emphasizes the
solution: The 'Shema' (Deuteronomy 6:4-9)—'Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one'—is the central creed of Israel that emphasizes the 'unity and oneness' of God.

options.b: richness of the spirit of God
options.d: crucial role o’ Israel in the world.

question: According to Galatians, the death of Christ has eliminate all forms of
solution: In Galatians 3:28, Paul explains that the unity in Christ has 'eliminated all forms of differences' between Jews and Greeks, slaves and free, and male and female.

question: In the Epistle to the Romans, justification is received through
solution: In Romans 5, Paul teaches that justification is 'received primarily through faith in Jesus Christ' (and not through the works of the law).

question: “Put me, I pray you, in one of the priest’s places, that I may eat a morsel of bread” Who made this prohecy and to whom was it made?
solution: In 1 Samuel 2:27-36, a 'Man of God' came to Eli and prophesied that the surviving members of his house would have to 'beg to eat a morsel of bread' due to their faithlessness.

options.b: A man of God to Eli

question: Paul in Timothy urged believers to make supplications, prayers, intercession and thanksgivings for all those who
solution: In 1 Timothy 2:1, Paul urges that prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for 'all men,' and specifically those 'who are in authority' (verse 2) to live peaceful and quiet lives.

question: Who among the following disciples of Jesus was a tax collector?
solution: In the Gospel accounts (Matthew 9:9 and Mark 2:14, where he is called 'Levi'), the disciple who was a 'tax collector' (publican) was Matthew/Levi.

question: In addition to the declaration of freedom to the exiles in Babylon, King Cyrus ordered for
solution: Cyrus Edict (Ezra 1:4-6) not only allowed the Jews to return but also encouraged 'neighbors/craftsmen in Babylon to support them' with silver, gold, and other precious goods for the temple.

options.a: free will offering for the house of God in Jerusalem
options.b: the release of Jewish, slaves in the empire
options.c: security check on the Jews before departure

question: The primary motive for Christian giving is to
solution: The primary biblical motive for Christian giving (2 Corinthians 9:7-15) is to 'reciprocate and express gratitude for God's blessings' and His inexpressible gift in Christ.

options.a: go to heaven when they die
options.c: support the work of the church.
options.d: provide for the servants of God

question: The Last Supper refers the last meal Jesus had with His disciples immediately before His_____
solution: The Last Supper (Matthew 26:17-29) was the final fellowship meal Jesus shared with His disciples before His 'betrayal and subsequent arrest' in Gethsemane.

question: God told Ezekiel that the house of Israel would not listen to him because they
solution: In Ezekiel 3:7, God warned the prophet that the house of Israel would not listen to him because they were 'of a stubborn heart and a hard forehead.'

options.a: were a people of foreign speech

question: The Judean King who revolted against Babylon after serving her for three years was
solution: 2 Kings 24:1 records that 'Jehoiakim' served the king of Babylon for three years and then 'revolted' against him.

question: The man who delivered judgement of the Jerusalem Council on the admission of Gentiles into the Church was
solution: Acts 15:13-21 records that 'James' (the brother of Jesus) delivered the final judgment and summary of the 'Jerusalem Council' regarding the Gentiles' admission.

question: Life in the Early Church was characterized by
solution: Acts 2:42-47 and 4:32-35 describes the life in the Early Church as characterized by a strong 'communal spirit' and sharing of all items.

question: Each of these spies sent out by Moses on God’s command to report on the nature of the promised land was a
solution: In Numbers 13:1-16, the spies sent by Moses were 'leaders representing each of the twelve tribes' of Israel.

question: Who was the last person Jesus appeared to after his resurrection according to Paul?
solution: In 1 Corinthians 15:8, Paul states that Jesus appeared to 'him' last of all, as to one untimely born.

question: The main message of Hosea is that God desires ___________.
solution: Hosea 6:6 is the pivotal verse where God declares His main message: 'I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice;' emphasizing inward devotion over outward ritual.

question: What yoke did the Council of Jerusalem say had been difficult for the Pharisees and their fathers to bear? The question of
solution: At the Council of Jerusalem (Acts 15:10), Peter argued that the yoke of 'circumcision and the Mosaic law' was something neither they nor their fathers could bear.

question: James teaches that all the following are true causes of unanswered prayers except
solution: James 4:2-3 lists several causes of unanswered prayer, but 'diligence' (a virtue) is 'not' listed as a cause of prayer failure like greed, wrong motives, or lack of faith.

question: The first batch of Jewish exiles to rebuild the temple was under the leadership of
solution: Ezra 1:11 and 2:1-2 states that the first batch of exiles to return and rebuild the temple was under the leadership of 'Sheshbazzar' (the prince) and later Zerubbabel.

question: The mother of Esau is
solution: In Genesis 24 and 25:19-21, 'Rebecca' (the daughter of Bethuel) is recorded as the wife of Isaac and the mother of the twins Jacob and Esau.

question: According to Daniel the command of Nebuchadnezzar to worship the golden image amounted to
solution: In Daniel 3, the refusal of the Hebrew men to worship the golden image highlighted that Nebuchadnezzar's command was a demand for 'idolatry' that violated God's law.

question: When Moses stayed away for too long on the mountain, the people of Israel
solution: Exodus 32:1-4 records that when Moses delayed coming down from Mount Sinai, the Israelites became impatient and pressured Aaron to 'make and worship a golden calf idol.'

question: The visitors of Peter after his vision at Joppa were from
solution: Acts 10:17-20 records that after Peter's vision at Joppa, the three men sent by the centurion Cornelius arrived to meet him from 'Caesarea.'

question: The Hebrew kingdom broke up because of
solution: 1 Kings 12:12-16 states that the Hebrew kingdom split primarily because 'Rehoboam refused to grant the Northerners' petition' for lighter taxes and service.

options.d: Rehoboam’s refusal to grant the Northerners’ petition

question: According to Jeremiah, the anger of God towards Israel would only be reduced by
solution: In the book of Jeremiah, the prophet emphasizes that despite Israel's persistent sins, God's anger would only be held back and reduced by 'His great mercy.'

question: Who among the following was to ensure the safety of King Saul in the wilderness of Ziph?
solution: 1 Samuel 26:5-7 records that 'Abner' (Saul's cousin and captain of the host) was the one responsible for the protection and safety of the king in the camp in the wilderness of Ziph.

question: The essence of St. Paul's teaching on Law and Grace is that___________
solution: The core of Paul's teaching on Law and Grace (Romans 3:20-28) is that 'observance of the law alone is not a guarantee of salvation,' which is received through faith in Christ.

options.a: observance of the law is necessary for redemption in Christ
options.b: what God truly rewards is the prayer for grace by Christians
options.c: observance of the law only isn't a guarantee of salvation
options.d: God's grace in Christians' lives shouldn't be a reason for continuing in sin

question: What shall I do with you, O Ephraim? What shall I do with you, O Judah? Your love is like a morning cloud...” In the statement above, the love of Israel is compared to a morning cloud because it
solution: In Hosea 6:4, God compares the inconsistent love of Israel (Ephraim) and Judah to a 'morning cloud' or 'dew that goes away early,' because it 'disappears fast' and is not lasting.

question: For worshipping the molten calf, God described the Israelites as
solution: In Exodus 32:9, after the people worshipped the golden calf, God described the Israelites to Moses as a 'stiff-necked people' (symbolizing their stubbornness and rebellion).

question: Jeremiah prophesied God's punishment of the Israelites because they had_______
solution: Jeremiah prophesied disaster for Judah primarily because they had 'compromised and corrupted the true worship of Yahweh' by turning to idols and injustice.

options.b: Committed atrocities with their neighbors' wives

question: A Christian can demonstrate that he is light of the world by___________
solution: In the Sermon on the Mount (Matthew 5:14-16), Jesus teaches that Christians are the 'light of the world' who should 'uphold good moral values' so others can see their good works.

options.a: Advocating for the meek to inherit the earth
