options.c: being merciful to persons in need

question: Amos declared that God hated solemn feasts, songs and sacrifices because the people of Israel were
solution: In Amos 5:21-24, God expressed hatred for Israel's religious festivals and sacrifices because the people were 'unjust and unrighteous,' lacking true social justice.

question: At about the ... hour, Jesus cried with a loud voice "Eli Eli lama sabach - thani".
solution: Matthew 27:46 records that at about the 'ninth hour' (3 PM), Jesus cried out from the cross: 'Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?'

question: The boy Moses was hidden for three months because
solution: Exodus 2:2 records that Moses' mother hid him for three months because she saw he was a 'fine/beautiful' child and feared Pharaoh's decree to 'murder all Hebrew male newborns.'

options.a: he was destined to become a great leader
options.b: he was attacked by an abominable disease
options.c: Pharaoh feared the growth of the Hebrew population
options.d: Pharaoh had decreed the murder of Hebrew male newborns

question: To be a good citizen, Peter advised believers to honour all men and
solution: In 1 Peter 2:17, as a guide to being a good citizen, the apostle gives four commands: 'Honor everyone. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the emperor.'

question: “Behold, if people of Israel are to come, let us deal shrewdly with them, lest they multiply.....” ln the statement, the first step by the king of Egypt was to
solution: Exodus 1:11 records that the first step taken by the new King of Egypt to deal with the Israelites was to 'set taskmasters over them' to afflict and burden them with forced labor.

options.a: cast all Hebrew male children into the Nile
options.d: set taskmasters over them to afflict them with burdens

question: By introducing Jesus as the Lamb, John was pointing to
solution: In John 1:29, when John the Baptist introduced Jesus as 'the Lamb of God,' he was primarily pointing to 'Jesus' sacrificial and redemptive death' for the sins of the world.

question: The two Hebrew midwives instructed by pharaoh to kill the Hebrew male babies at birth were:
solution: Exodus 1:15 identifies the two Hebrew midwives who were commanded by Pharaoh to kill the male babies as 'Shiphrah and Puah.'

question: The agony of Jesus in the Garden of Gethsemane is a
solution: Mathew 26:36-46 records that Jesus' deep agony in the Garden of Gethsemane was his final spiritual 'preparation for His death' and His submission to the Father's will.

question: Paul taught the Colossians that the relationship between wives and their husbands should be as
solution: In Colossians 3:18, Paul teaches that wives should be subject to their husbands as is 'fitting in the Lord,' framing marriage within Christian devotion.

question: The Amalekites who opposed the Israelites in the wilderness were from the lineage of
solution: Exodus 17:8-16 records the battle with the Amalekites; traditions from Genesis 36 trace the Amalekites back to the lineage of 'Esau' (Jacob's brother).

question: “No one can serve two masters...” This means that one should not
solution: Jesus' teaching 'No one can serve two masters' (Matthew 6:24) emphasizes that one cannot worship both God and 'Mammon' (money/idols), warning against 'idolatry.'

question: Jesus demonstrated his compassion for humanity in all the following miracles except the
solution: While Jesus' miracles generally show compassion, the 'healing of the centurion's servant' (Matthew 8:5-13) is often categorized as a miracle of faith rather than one primarily highlighting compassion' (Checking JAMB preference).

question: Nebuchadnezzar allowed the Chaldeans, Syrians and Moabites to attack Judah so that Judah might
solution: 2 Kings 24:2 states that the Lord sent Chaldeans, Syrians, and Moabites against Judah to 'destroy and remove Judah' from His sight because of the sins of Manasseh.

question: Through God the Christian is no longer a slave but a son, and if a son then
solution: In Galatians 4:7, Paul writes: 'So you are no longer a slave, but a son, and if a son, then an heir through God.'

question: Sin that reigns in our mortal bodies makes us obey
solution: In Romans 6:12, Paul warns that if sin is allowed to reign in our mortal bodies, it makes us 'obey our sinful passions and desires.'

question:
Which of the following, accordings to James is an important
 factor for effective prayer?
solution: James 1:6 and 5:15 list 'Faith' as the most critical and important factor for the effectiveness of prayer.

question: Paul returned Philemon’s slave back to him, to ensure that
solution: In his letter to Philemon (verse 12-14), Paul returned the slave Onesimus primarily so that any act of 'forgiveness and acceptance by Philemon' would be voluntary and not by compulsion.

options.a: Philemon forgave and accepted the slave
options.b: the slave can complete his service time
options.c: he would be welcome when he left prison
options.d: he could boast of Philemon’s obedience

question: One significance of St Paul's teaching on Resurrection is that__________
solution: 1 Corinthians 15 states that the 'foundation of the Christian hope of salvation' is built entirely on the reality of 'Christ's resurrection.'

options.a: Christians must not underestimate the power of baptism by immersion
options.b: As Christ rose and ascended into glory, so as also must uphold the observance of law
options.c: The foundation of their hope of salvation is in Christ's resurrection
options.d: The second coming of Christ is near

question: At the time of creation, the river that flowed out of Eden to water the garden divided into
solution: Genesis 2:10-14 names the four rivers flowing out of Eden as 'Pishon, Gihon, Tigris (Hiddekel), and Euphrates.'

question: He shall save his people from their sins. Who is being referred to here?
solution: Matthew 1:21 records the angel telling Joseph that Mary will have a son and he shall call His name 'Jesus,' for 'He will save His people from their sins.'

question: Noah’s ark was sustained during the flood by its
solution: Genesis 7:17-18 states that during the great flood, 'Noah's ark' was sustained and moved by 'floating/drifting safely upon the waters.'

question: The miracles of Jesus in St. John’s Gospel are referred to as signs’ of the
solution: In John's Gospel, Jesus' miracles are called 'signs' because they are 'outward manifestations of Jesus' identity as the Messiah' and the Son of God.

options.a: manifestations of Jesus as the Messiah
options.c: judgment that will come upon unbelievers

question: The arrest of Jesus by his enemies was
solution: Matthew 26:54-56 states that the arrest of Jesus in Gethsemane occurred so that the 'prophecies of the Scriptures might be fulfilled.'

options.b: a result of his tough teaching
options.d: a ploy to see his miraculous powers

question: “It is the Lord, let Him do what seems good to Him”. These were the words of
solution: 1 Samuel 3:18 records that when Samuel told Eli the hard message of judgment, 'Eli' resignedly said: 'It is the Lord; let Him do what seems good to Him.'

question: The appointment of deacons in the Early Church was meant to check
solution: Acts 6:1-3 states that the seven deacons were appointed primarily to 'ensure fairness and check discrimination' in the daily distribution of food to widows.

question: God prepared Joseph for his leadership role in Egypt through
solution: The biblical account of Joseph (Genesis 37-50) shows that God used Joseph's 'trials, betrayal, and temptations' to prepare and position him for leadership in Egypt.

options.a: the love which his father had for him.
options.b: the trials and temptations of his earlier years.

question: During the rebuilding of the temple, Zerubbabel rejected the offer of help from the
solution: Ezra 4:1-3 records that Zerubbabel and the leaders 'rejected the offer of help from the Samaritans' (the adversaries of Judah and Benjamin) in rebuilding the temple.

options.d: governors of the provinces beyond the rivers

question: In Corinthians, Paul said that one can only say Jesus is Lord by
solution: In 1 Corinthians 12:3, Paul states that 'no one can say Jesus is Lord except by the Holy Spirit.'

question: “ ..... I will surely tear the kingdom from you and will give it to your servant .....” An unwise decision of Solomon that resulted in God making the pronouncement above was his
solution: 1 Kings 11:11 records that God told Solomon He would tear the kingdom away because Solomon had 'neglected God for other gods' through his foreign wives.

options.a: dependence on other nations for supplies
options.c: neglect of God for other gods

question: When David learnt that the child born to him by Bathsheba had died, he ____________,
solution: 2 Samuel 12:20 records that after his child died, David ceased fasting, 'anointed himself, changed his clothes, and went to the house of the Lord and worshipped.'

options.a: fasted and prayed for the dead
options.b: tore his robe and dismissed his court
options.c: anointed himself and worshipped the Lord
options.d: sought Nathan’s counsel on what to do next

question: Joel and Abijah were the sons of
solution: 1 Samuel 8:2 identifies the sons of Samuel as 'Joel and Abijah,' whom he appointed as judges in Beersheba.

question: Which of these was detained by Joseph in order that Benjamin might be brought to Egypt
solution: In Genesis 42:24, Joseph took 'Simeon' (Simon) from among his brothers and bound him to ensure they would return with Benjamin.

question: Apart from the miracles at the wedding feast, another miracle of Jesus at Cana in Galilee was
solution: John 4:46-54 records that the 'second sign' Jesus did at Cana (after the wedding) was the 'healing of the official's/noble man's son' who was sick in Capernaum.

options.c: healing of a man born blind

question: "Who do men say that the Son of man is?" Jesus asked this question
solution: In Matthew 16:13-16, Jesus asked 'Who do men say that the Son of man is?' in the context of leading up to 'Peter's Great Confession' of Him as the Christ.

options.a: to show that popular opinion matters the most in His recognition as Messiah
options.b: to indicate that the Jews and Pharisees need to reckon with His Messiahship
options.c: at the scene of His Transfiguration
options.d: in the context of the Great Confession

question: According to Peter, a Christian should exercise his freedom by
solution: In 1 Peter 2:16, the apostle advises that Christians should live as free people but should use their 'freedom to do what is right (godly service)' rather than as a pretext for evil.

options.b: claiming his freedom at all cost
options.d: using it as a pretext for evil

question: According to Matthew’s account of the baptism of Jesus, the voice from heaven said
solution: In Matthew's account of the baptism (Matthew 3:17), the voice from heaven said: 'This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.'

question: James taught that Christians should offer prayers of thanksgiving when they are
solution: James 5:13 asks: 'Is any among you suffering? Let him pray. Is any cheerful? Let him sing praise' (offering prayers of thanksgiving/cheer).

question: Which of the following miracles shows Jesus’ power over nature?
solution: Jesus' miracles like 'walking on the sea' (Matthew 14:25) and 'calming the storm' are clear demonstrations of His 'divine power over nature.'

options.c: Healing of the woman with issue of blood
options.d: Raising of Lazarus from the dead

question: Joshua’s first victory was over the people of
solution: In Joshua 6, 'Jericho' was the first city and nation that the Israelites conquered under Joshua's leadership after crossing the Jordan.

question: Which of the following incidents occurred during Jesus’ trial before Caiaphas?
solution: Matthew 26:59-60 records that during Jesus' trial before Caiaphas, the chief priests and the whole council 'kept seeking false witness against Jesus' to put Him to death.

options.a: The preference of Barabbas to Jesus
options.b: The question of payment of tribute
options.c: Accusation of Jesus as a rioter
options.d: The seeking of false witness against Jesus

question: According to Paul in his letter to Philemon, forgiveness should not be by compulsion but by
solution: In his letter to Philemon (verse 14), Paul emphasized that He wantsed Philemon's 'good deed and forgiveness to be of his own free will' rather than by necessity or compulsion.
